1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Vector Index Store
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
5: // SEARCH: VECTOR INDEX
6: // TypeScript port of the vector index implementation.
7: // DECAY STRATEGY: Search-time temporal decay uses an
8: // FSRS-preferred strategy. Memories with FSRS review data (last_review
9: // IS NOT NULL, review_count > 0) use the FSRS v4 power-law formula:
10: // R(t) = (1 + 0.2346 * t / S)^(-0.5)
11: // Memories without review data fall back to half-life exponential:
12: // Weight * 0.5^(days / half_life_days)
13: // This ensures backward compatibility while aligning reviewed
14: // Memories with the canonical FSRS algorithm.

87: function loadSearchWeights(): SearchWeightsConfig {
88:   // SERVER_DIR points to dist/ at runtime; configs/ lives at the package root (dist/..)
89:   const candidates = [
90:     path.join(SERVER_DIR, 'configs', 'search-weights.json'),
91:     path.join(SERVER_DIR, '..', 'configs', 'search-weights.json'),
92:   ];
93:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
94:     try {
95:       return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(candidate, 'utf-8')) as SearchWeightsConfig;
96:     } catch {
97:       // Try next candidate
98:     }
99:   }
100:   return {};
101: }

146: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147:    1. CONFIGURATION — Embedding Dimension
148: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
149: 
150: /** Default embedding dimension used by the vector index. */
151: export const EMBEDDING_DIM = getEmbeddingDimension();
152: 
153: /**
154:  * Gets the active embedding dimension for the current provider.
155:  * @returns The embedding dimension.
156:  */
157: export function get_embedding_dim(): number {
158:   return getEmbeddingDimension();
159: }
160: 
161: /**
162:  * Waits for the embedding provider to report a stable dimension.
163:  * @param timeout_ms - The maximum time to wait in milliseconds.
164:  * @returns A promise that resolves to the confirmed embedding dimension.
165:  */
166: export async function get_confirmed_embedding_dimension(timeout_ms = 5000): Promise<number> {
167:   const start = Date.now();
168:   while (Date.now() - start < timeout_ms) {
169:     const dim = get_embedding_dim();
170:     if (Number.isFinite(dim) && dim > 0) {
171:       return dim;
172:     }
173:     await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
174:   }
175:   const fallbackDim = get_embedding_dim();
176:   console.warn(`[vector-index] Using embedding dimension ${fallbackDim} after timeout`);
177:   return fallbackDim;
178: }
179: 
180: type EmbeddingDimensionValidation = {
181:   valid: boolean;
182:   stored: number | null;
183:   current: number | null;
184:   reason?: string;
185:   warning?: string;
186: };
187: 
188: type StoredEmbeddingDimension = {
189:   existing_db: boolean;
190:   stored_dim: number | null;
191:   source: 'vec_metadata' | 'vec_memories' | null;
192:   reason?: string;
193: };
194: 
195: function get_existing_embedding_dimension(
196:   database: Database.Database,
197: ): StoredEmbeddingDimension {
198:   const schema_row = database.prepare(`
199:     SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_index'
200:   `).get();
201: 
202:   if (!schema_row) {
203:     return {
204:       existing_db: false,
205:       stored_dim: null,
206:       source: null,
207:       reason: 'No existing schema',
208:     };
209:   }
210: 
211:   const attached = database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string }>;
212:   if (attached.some((entry) => entry.name === ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA)) {
213:     const shard_metadata_table = database.prepare(`
214:       SELECT name FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
215:     `).get();
216: 
217:     if (shard_metadata_table) {
218:       const stored_row = database.prepare(`
219:         SELECT value FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata WHERE key IN ('embedding_dim', 'dim')
220:         ORDER BY CASE key WHEN 'embedding_dim' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END
221:         LIMIT 1
222:       `).get() as { value: string } | undefined;
223: 
224:       if (stored_row) {
225:         const stored_dim = parseInt(stored_row.value, 10);
226:         if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
227:           return {
228:             existing_db: true,
229:             stored_dim,
230:             source: 'vec_metadata',
231:           };
232:         }
233:       }
234:     }
235:   }
236: 
237:   const metadata_table = database.prepare(`
238:     SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
239:   `).get();
240: 
241:   if (metadata_table) {
242:     const stored_row = database.prepare(`
243:       SELECT value FROM vec_metadata WHERE key = 'embedding_dim'
244:     `).get() as { value: string } | undefined;
245: 
246:     if (stored_row) {
247:       const stored_dim = parseInt(stored_row.value, 10);
248:       if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
249:         return {
250:           existing_db: true,
251:           stored_dim,
252:           source: 'vec_metadata',
253:         };
254:       }
255:     }
256:   }
257: 
258:   const vec_table = database.prepare(`
259:     SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE name='vec_memories'
260:   `).get() as { sql: string | null } | undefined;
261:   const vec_sql = typeof vec_table?.sql === 'string' ? vec_table.sql : null;
262:   const match = vec_sql?.match(/FLOAT\[(\d+)\]/i);
263: 
264:   if (match) {
265:     const stored_dim = parseInt(match[1], 10);
266:     if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
267:       return {
268:         existing_db: true,
269:         stored_dim,
270:         source: 'vec_memories',
271:       };
272:     }
273:   }
274: 
275:   return {
276:     existing_db: true,
277:     stored_dim: null,
278:     source: null,
279:     reason: 'No stored vector dimension found for existing schema',
280:   };
281: }
282: 
283: function validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(
284:   database: Database.Database | null,
285:   _sqlite_vec_available: boolean,
286: ): EmbeddingDimensionValidation {
287:   if (!database) {
288:     return { valid: true, stored: null, current: null, reason: 'No database' };
289:   }
290: 
291:   try {
292:     const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
293:     const current_dim = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && active.dim > 0
294:       ? active.dim
295:       : get_embedding_dim();
296:     const existing = get_existing_embedding_dimension(database);
297: 
298:     if (!existing.existing_db) {
299:       return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason };
300:     }
301: 
302:     if (existing.stored_dim == null) {
303:       return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason || 'No stored dimension' };
304:     }
305: 
306:     if (existing.stored_dim !== current_dim) {
307:       const source_label = existing.source === 'vec_metadata' ? 'vec_metadata' : 'vec_memories schema';
308:       const warning = `EMBEDDING DIMENSION MISMATCH: Existing database stores ${existing.stored_dim}-dim vectors (${source_label}), ` +
309:         `but the active embedding configuration resolves to ${current_dim}. Refusing to bootstrap because vector search would fail. ` +
310:         `Solutions: 1) Delete database and re-index, 2) Set EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER to match original, ` +
311:         `3) Let SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR auto-derive a profile-specific database, or set MEMORY_DB_PATH only for an intentional file override.`;
312:       console.error(`[vector-index] WARNING: ${warning}`);
313:       return { valid: false, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim, warning };
314:     }
315: 
316:     return { valid: true, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim };
317:   } catch (e: unknown) {
318:     console.warn('[vector-index] Dimension validation error:', get_error_message(e));
319:     return { valid: true, stored: null, current: get_embedding_dim(), reason: get_error_message(e) };
320:   }
321: }
322: 
323: /**
324:  * Validates that stored vector dimensions match the provider.
325:  * @returns The validation result.

328:   return validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(db, sqlite_vec_available_flag);

396: function resolve_profile_for_active_embedder(database: Database.Database): EmbeddingProfile {
397:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
398:   if (active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && active.dim > 0) {
399:     const startup = getStartupEmbeddingProfile();
400:     return new EmbeddingProfile({
401:       provider: active.provider ?? startup.provider,
402:       model: active.name,
403:       dim: active.dim,
404:       dtype: null,
405:       baseUrl: startup.baseUrl,

407:   }
408:   return getStartupEmbeddingProfile();

429: function table_exists_in_schema(database: Database.Database, schema: string, tableName: string): boolean {
430:   const row = database.prepare(`
431:     SELECT 1 AS found
432:     FROM ${quote_identifier(schema)}.sqlite_master
433:     WHERE type IN ('table', 'view') AND name = ?
434:     LIMIT 1
435:   `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
436:   return row?.found === 1;
437: }

439: function write_shard_metadata(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
440:   database.exec(`
441:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata (
442:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
443:       value TEXT NOT NULL,
444:       created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
445:     )
446:   `);
447: 
448:   const rows = database.prepare(`
449:     SELECT key, value
450:     FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata
451:     WHERE key IN ('provider', 'model', 'dim', 'embedding_dim')
452:   `).all() as Array<{ key: string; value: string }>;
453:   const metadata = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.key, row.value]));
454:   const mismatches = [
455:     ['provider', profile.provider],
456:     ['model', profile.model],
457:     ['dim', String(profile.dim)],
458:     ['embedding_dim', String(profile.dim)],
459:   ].filter(([key, expected]) => {
460:     const actual = metadata.get(key);
461:     return actual != null && actual.length > 0 && actual !== expected;
462:   });
463: 
464:   if (mismatches.length > 0) {
465:     const detail = mismatches.map(([key, expected]) => `${key}: expected ${expected}, got ${metadata.get(key)}`).join('; ');
466:     throw new VectorIndexError(
467:       `Attached vector shard metadata mismatch (${detail})`,
468:       VectorIndexErrorCode.INTEGRITY_ERROR,
469:     );
470:   }
471: 
472:   const upsert = database.prepare(`
473:     INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata (key, value)
474:     VALUES (?, ?)
475:   `);
476:   upsert.run('provider', profile.provider);
477:   upsert.run('model', profile.model);
478:   upsert.run('dim', String(profile.dim));
479:   upsert.run('embedding_dim', String(profile.dim));
480: }
481: 
482: function ensure_embedding_cache_table(database: Database.Database): void {
483:   database.exec(`
484:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.embedding_cache (
485:       content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
486:       profile_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
487:       input_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'document' CHECK (input_kind IN ('document', 'query')),
488:       model_id TEXT NOT NULL,
489:       embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
490:       dimensions INTEGER NOT NULL,
491:       created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
492:       last_used_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
493:       PRIMARY KEY (content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, dimensions)
494:     )
495:   `);
496: }

498: function ensure_vector_shard_schema(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
499:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.journal_mode = WAL`);
500:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.cache_size = -8192`);
501:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.mmap_size = 33554432`);
502:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.temp_store = DEFAULT`);
503:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_autocheckpoint = 256`);
504: 
505:   write_shard_metadata(database, profile);
506:   ensure_embedding_cache_table(database);
507: 
508:   const dimTableName = vector_table_name_for_profile(profile);
509:   database.exec(`
510:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)} (
511:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
512:       vec BLOB NOT NULL
513:     )
514:   `);
515: 
516:   if (sqlite_vec_available_flag && !table_exists_in_schema(database, ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA, 'vec_memories')) {
517:     database.exec(`
518:       CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories USING vec0(
519:         embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
520:       )
521:     `);
522:   }
523: }

525: function drop_canonical_vector_payload_tables(database: Database.Database): void {
526:   for (const tableName of ['embedding_cache', 'vec_memories']) {
527:     try {
528:       database.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS main.${quote_identifier(tableName)}`);
529:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
530:       // Existing legacy virtual/shadow tables are migrated before this cleanup; failures are non-fatal.
531:     }
532:   }
533:   const rows = database.prepare(`
534:     SELECT name
535:     FROM main.sqlite_master
536:     WHERE type='table'
537:       AND (name LIKE 'vec\\_%' ESCAPE '\\' OR name LIKE 'vec_memories\\_%' ESCAPE '\\')
538:   `).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
539:   for (const row of rows) {
540:     if (row.name === 'vec_metadata') {
541:       continue;
542:     }
543:     try {
544:       database.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS main.${quote_identifier(row.name)}`);
545:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
546:       // Best-effort canonical slimming. Query paths use active_vec after attach.
547:     }
548:   }
549: }

551: function create_legacy_temp_aliases(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
552:   const dimTableName = vector_table_name_for_profile(profile);
553:   try {
554:     database.exec(`
555:       DROP VIEW IF EXISTS temp.vec_memories;
556:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.vec_memories_insert;
557:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.vec_memories_delete;
558:       CREATE TEMP VIEW vec_memories AS
559:         SELECT rowid, embedding FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories;
560:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER vec_memories_insert
561:       INSTEAD OF INSERT ON vec_memories
562:       BEGIN
563:         INSERT INTO ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories(rowid, embedding)
564:         VALUES (CAST(new.rowid AS INTEGER), new.embedding);
565:       END;
566:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER vec_memories_delete
567:       INSTEAD OF DELETE ON vec_memories
568:       BEGIN
569:         DELETE FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories WHERE rowid = old.rowid;
570:       END;
571:       DROP VIEW IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)};
572:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_insert`)};
573:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_delete`)};
574:       CREATE TEMP VIEW ${quote_identifier(dimTableName)} AS
575:         SELECT id, vec FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)};
576:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER ${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_insert`)}
577:       INSTEAD OF INSERT ON ${quote_identifier(dimTableName)}
578:       BEGIN
579:         INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)}(id, vec)
580:         VALUES (new.id, new.vec);
581:       END;
582:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER ${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_delete`)}
583:       INSTEAD OF DELETE ON ${quote_identifier(dimTableName)}
584:       BEGIN
585:         DELETE FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)} WHERE id = old.id;
586:       END;
587:     `);
588:   } catch (error: unknown) {
589:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Could not create legacy vector temp aliases: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
590:   }
591: }
592: 
593: function drop_legacy_temp_aliases(database: Database.Database): void {
594:   try {
595:     const rows = database.prepare(`
596:       SELECT type, name
597:       FROM temp.sqlite_master
598:       WHERE name = 'vec_memories'
599:          OR name LIKE 'vec_memories\\_%' ESCAPE '\\'
600:          OR name LIKE 'vec\\_%' ESCAPE '\\'
601:     `).all() as Array<{ type: string; name: string }>;
602: 
603:     for (const row of rows) {
604:       if (row.type !== 'view' && row.type !== 'trigger') {
605:         continue;
606:       }
607:       database.exec(`DROP ${row.type.toUpperCase()} IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(row.name)}`);
608:     }
609:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
610:     // Best-effort cleanup only.
611:   }
612: }
613: 
614: // Unified allowed paths
615: const ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS = [
616:   path.join(process.cwd(), 'specs'),
617:   path.join(process.cwd(), '.opencode'),
618:   path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude'),
619:   process.cwd(),
620:   ...(process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS ? process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS.split(path.delimiter) : [])
621: ].filter(Boolean).map(p => path.resolve(p));
622: 
623: /**
624:  * Validates a file path against allowed local base paths.
625:  * @param file_path - The file path to validate.
626:  * @returns The validated file path, if allowed.
627:  */
628: export function validate_file_path_local(file_path: unknown): string | null {
629:   if (typeof file_path !== 'string') {
630:     return null;
631:   }
632: 
633:   return validateFilePath(file_path, ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS);
634: }
635: 
636: // HIGH-004 FIX: Async version for non-blocking concurrent file reads
637: /**
638:  * Reads a file asynchronously after validating the path.
639:  * @param file_path - The file path to read.
640:  * @returns A promise that resolves to the file contents or an empty string.
641:  */
642: export async function safe_read_file_async(file_path: unknown): Promise<string> {
643:   const valid_path = validate_file_path_local(file_path);
644:   if (!valid_path) {
645:     return '';
646:   }
647: 
648:   try {
649:     return await fs.promises.readFile(valid_path, 'utf-8');
650:   } catch (err: unknown) {
651:     if (!(err instanceof Error && 'code' in err && get_error_code(err) === 'ENOENT')) {
652:       console.warn(`[vector-index] Could not read file ${valid_path}: ${get_error_message(err)}`);
653:     }
654:     return '';
655:   }
656: }
657: 
658: // Safely parse JSON with validation (CWE-502: Deserialization mitigation)
659: /**
660:  * Parses JSON with prototype-pollution safeguards.
661:  * @param json_string - The JSON string to parse.
662:  * @param default_value - The fallback value to return on failure.
663:  * @returns The parsed JSON value or the fallback.
664:  */
665: export function safe_parse_json(json_string: unknown, default_value = []): unknown {
666:   if (!json_string || typeof json_string !== 'string') {
667:     return default_value;
668:   }
669: 
670:   try {
671:     const parsed = JSON.parse(json_string);
672: 
673:     if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
674:       return parsed.filter(item =>
675:         item && typeof item === 'object' &&
676:         !Array.isArray(item) &&
677:         !('__proto__' in item) &&
678:         !('constructor' in item) &&
679:         !('prototype' in item)
680:       );
681:     }
682: 
683:     if (typeof parsed === 'object' && parsed !== null) {
684:       if ('__proto__' in parsed || 'constructor' in parsed || 'prototype' in parsed) {
685:         console.warn('[vector-index] Blocked potential prototype pollution in JSON');
686:         return default_value;
687:       }
688:     }
689: 
690:     return parsed;
691:   } catch (err: unknown) {
692:     console.warn(`[vector-index] JSON parse error: ${get_error_message(err)}`);
693:     return default_value;
694:   }
695: }
696: 
697: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
698:    3. DATABASE SINGLETON
699: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
700: 
701: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
702: let db_path = resolve_database_path();
703: let sqlite_vec_available_flag = true;
704: let active_vector_source: ActiveVectorSourceTelemetry | null = null;
705: // C1 FIX: Key connections by resolved DB path to prevent cross-store data corruption
706: const db_connections = new Map<string, Database.Database>();
707: const UNCLEAN_SHUTDOWN_MARKER = '.unclean-shutdown';
708: type DatabaseConnectionListener = (
709:   database: Database.Database,
710:   change: {
711:     previousDb: Database.Database | null;
712:     previousPath: string;
713:     nextPath: string;
714:   },
715: ) => boolean | void;
716: const database_connection_listeners = new Set<DatabaseConnectionListener>();
717: 
718: function set_active_database_connection(
719:   connection: Database.Database,
720:   target_path: string,
721:   vec_available: boolean,
722: ): void {
723:   const previousDb = db;
724:   const previousPath = db_path;
725:   db = connection;
726:   db_path = target_path;
727:   sqlite_vec_available_flag = vec_available;
728: 
729:   if (previousDb !== connection || previousPath !== target_path) {
730:     clear_constitutional_cache();
731: 
732:     const listenerErrors: string[] = [];
733:     for (const listener of database_connection_listeners) {
734:       try {
735:         const listenerResult = listener(connection, {
736:           previousDb,
737:           previousPath,
738:           nextPath: target_path,
739:         });
740:         if (listenerResult === false) {
741:           listenerErrors.push('listener returned false');
742:         }
743:       } catch (error: unknown) {
744:         const message = get_error_message(error);
745:         listenerErrors.push(message);
746:         console.warn(`[vector-index] Database connection listener failed: ${message}`);
747:       }
748:     }
749:     if (listenerErrors.length > 0) {
750:       throw new Error(`Database connection listener rebind failed: ${listenerErrors.join('; ')}`);
751:     }
752:   }
753: 
754:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
755:     return;
756:   }
757: 
758:   try {
759:     fs.chmodSync(target_path, DB_PERMISSIONS);
760:   } catch (err: unknown) {
761:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Could not set permissions on ${target_path}: ${get_error_message(err)}`);
762:   }
763: }
764: 
765: function get_unclean_shutdown_marker_path(target_path: string): string | null {
766:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
767:     return null;
768:   }
769:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), UNCLEAN_SHUTDOWN_MARKER);
770: }
771: 
772: function write_unclean_shutdown_marker(target_path: string): void {
773:   const marker_path = get_unclean_shutdown_marker_path(target_path);
774:   if (!marker_path) {
775:     return;
776:   }
777: 
778:   try {
779:     fs.writeFileSync(marker_path, `${JSON.stringify({
780:       pid: process.pid,
781:       databasePath: target_path,
782:       startedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
783:     })}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
784:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
785:     // Best-effort: marker failure must not block DB startup.
786:   }
787: }
788: 
789: function remove_unclean_shutdown_marker(target_path: string): void {
790:   const marker_path = get_unclean_shutdown_marker_path(target_path);
791:   if (!marker_path) {
792:     return;
793:   }
794: 
795:   try {
796:     fs.rmSync(marker_path, { force: true });
797:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
798:     // Best-effort: marker cleanup must not block DB close.
799:   }
800: }
801: 
802: function remove_sqlite_sidecars(database_path: string): void {
803:   for (const sidecar_path of [`${database_path}-wal`, `${database_path}-shm`]) {
804:     fs.rmSync(sidecar_path, { force: true });
805:   }
806: }
807: 
808: function fsync_file_if_possible(file_path: string): void {
809:   let fd: number | null = null;
810:   try {
811:     fd = fs.openSync(file_path, 'r');
812:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
813:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
814:     // Recovery remains journal-driven when the platform cannot flush explicitly.
815:   } finally {
816:     if (fd !== null) {
817:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
818:     }
819:   }
820: }
821: 
822: function fsync_directory_if_possible(dir_path: string): void {
823:   let fd: number | null = null;
824:   try {
825:     fd = fs.openSync(dir_path, 'r');
826:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
827:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
828:     // Directory fsync is unavailable on some platforms/filesystems.
829:   } finally {
830:     if (fd !== null) {
831:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
832:     }
833:   }
834: }
835: 
836: function get_restore_journal_path(target_path: string): string | null {
837:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
838:     return null;
839:   }
840:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), 'checkpoints', RESTORE_JOURNAL_NAME);
841: }
842: 
843: function get_needs_rebuild_sentinel_path(target_path: string): string | null {
844:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
845:     return null;
846:   }
847:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), 'checkpoints', NEEDS_REBUILD_SENTINEL_NAME);
848: }
849: 
850: function write_needs_rebuild_sentinel_for_recovered_restore(target_path: string, checkpoint_name: string): void {
851:   const sentinel_path = get_needs_rebuild_sentinel_path(target_path);
852:   if (!sentinel_path || fs.existsSync(sentinel_path)) {
853:     return;
854:   }
855: 
856:   try {
857:     const sentinel_dir = path.dirname(sentinel_path);
858:     const temp_sentinel_path = `${sentinel_path}.tmp`;
859:     fs.mkdirSync(sentinel_dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
860:     fs.writeFileSync(temp_sentinel_path, `${JSON.stringify({
861:       formatVersion: 1,
862:       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
863:       source: 'swap_done_recovery',
864:       reason: 'completed restore journal recovered without derived rebuild evidence',
865:       checkpointName: checkpoint_name,
866:     }, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
867:     fsync_file_if_possible(temp_sentinel_path);
868:     fs.renameSync(temp_sentinel_path, sentinel_path);
869:     fsync_directory_if_possible(sentinel_dir);
870:   } catch (error: unknown) {
871:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to write checkpoint needs-rebuild sentinel during recovery: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
872:   }
873: }
874: 
875: function write_needs_rebuild_sentinel_for_corruption(target_path: string, detail: string): void {
876:   // Same atomic temp+rename discipline as the restore-recovery writer: a crash
877:   // mid-write must never leave a torn sentinel that half-triggers a rebuild.
878:   const sentinel_path = get_needs_rebuild_sentinel_path(target_path);
879:   if (!sentinel_path) {
880:     return;
881:   }
882:   try {
883:     const sentinel_dir = path.dirname(sentinel_path);
884:     fs.mkdirSync(sentinel_dir, { recursive: true });
885:     const temp_sentinel_path = `${sentinel_path}.tmp.${process.pid}`;
886:     fs.writeFileSync(temp_sentinel_path, `${JSON.stringify({
887:       formatVersion: 1,
888:       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
889:       source: 'post_crash_integrity_probe',
890:       reason: detail,
891:     }, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
892:     fsync_file_if_possible(temp_sentinel_path);
893:     fs.renameSync(temp_sentinel_path, sentinel_path);
894:     fsync_directory_if_possible(sentinel_dir);
895:   } catch (error: unknown) {
896:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to write needs-rebuild sentinel after integrity probe: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
897:   }
898: }
899: 
900: function read_restore_journal(journal_path: string): RestoreJournalFile | null {
901:   try {
902:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(journal_path, 'utf-8')) as Partial<RestoreJournalFile>;
903:     const phase = parsed.phase === 'swap-done' ? 'swap-done' : 'swap-pending';
904:     if (
905:       parsed.formatVersion !== 1
906:       || typeof parsed.createdAt !== 'string'
907:       || typeof parsed.checkpointName !== 'string'
908:       || typeof parsed.liveMainPath !== 'string'
909:       || typeof parsed.backupMainPath !== 'string'
910:       || typeof parsed.snapshotMainPath !== 'string'
911:       || (parsed.liveShardPath !== null && typeof parsed.liveShardPath !== 'string')
912:       || (parsed.backupShardPath !== null && typeof parsed.backupShardPath !== 'string')
913:       || (parsed.snapshotVecPath !== null && typeof parsed.snapshotVecPath !== 'string')
914:       || typeof parsed.shouldRestoreVec !== 'boolean'
915:       || (
916:         parsed.liveShardPreexisted !== undefined
917:         && typeof parsed.liveShardPreexisted !== 'boolean'
918:       )
919:     ) {
920:       throw new Error('restore journal shape is invalid');
921:     }
922:     return {
923:       ...parsed,
924:       phase,
925:       liveShardPreexisted: parsed.liveShardPreexisted
926:         ?? (parsed.shouldRestoreVec && typeof parsed.backupShardPath === 'string' && fs.existsSync(parsed.backupShardPath)),
927:     } as RestoreJournalFile;
928:   } catch (error: unknown) {
929:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Ignoring unreadable checkpoint restore journal: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
930:     return null;
931:   }
932: }
933: 
934: function merge_checkpoint_catalog_from_backup(live_main_path: string, backup_main_path: string): void {
935:   // A swap-done crash happens after the snapshot DB is in place but before the live
936:   // catalog rows (captured from the pre-swap DB) are re-merged in-process. The snapshot's
937:   // checkpoints table only reflects state at snapshot time, so post-snapshot catalog rows
938:   // would be lost when boot recovery deletes the .bak. Re-merge them here, reading the BLOB
939:   // payloads directly from the .bak so v1 inline snapshots keep full fidelity. Best-effort:
940:   // recovery must finalize even if the merge cannot run.
941:   if (!fs.existsSync(backup_main_path)) {
942:     return;
943:   }
944:   let live_db: Database.Database | null = null;
945:   let backup_db: Database.Database | null = null;
946:   try {
947:     backup_db = new Database(backup_main_path, { readonly: true });
948:     const catalog_exists = backup_db.prepare(
949:       "SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'table' AND name = 'checkpoints'",
950:     ).get() as { 1?: number } | undefined;
951:     if (!catalog_exists) {
952:       return;
953:     }
954:     const backup_rows = backup_db.prepare('SELECT * FROM checkpoints ORDER BY id ASC').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
955:     if (backup_rows.length === 0) {
956:       return;
957:     }
958: 
959:     live_db = new Database(live_main_path);
960:     const live_catalog_exists = live_db.prepare(
961:       "SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'table' AND name = 'checkpoints'",
962:     ).get() as { 1?: number } | undefined;
963:     if (!live_catalog_exists) {
964:       return;
965:     }
966:     const target_columns = new Set(
967:       (live_db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info("checkpoints")').all() as Array<{ name?: unknown }>)
968:         .map((column) => column.name)
969:         .filter((name): name is string => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0),
970:     );
971: 
972:     const merge = live_db.transaction((rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>) => {
973:       for (const row of rows) {
974:         const name = row.name;
975:         if (typeof name !== 'string' || name.length === 0) {
976:           continue;
977:         }
978:         const existing_by_name = (live_db as Database.Database).prepare('SELECT id FROM checkpoints WHERE name = ?').get(name) as { id?: number } | undefined;
979:         if (existing_by_name?.id) {
980:           continue;
981:         }
982: 
983:         let columns = Object.keys(row).filter((column) => target_columns.has(column));
984:         const id = row.id;
985:         if (typeof id === 'number') {
986:           const existing_by_id = (live_db as Database.Database).prepare('SELECT name FROM checkpoints WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { name?: string } | undefined;
987:           if (existing_by_id?.name && existing_by_id.name !== name) {
988:             columns = columns.filter((column) => column !== 'id');
989:           }
990:         }
991:         if (columns.length === 0) {
992:           continue;
993:         }
994: 
995:         const placeholders = columns.map(() => '?').join(', ');
996:         (live_db as Database.Database).prepare(`
997:           INSERT OR IGNORE INTO checkpoints (${columns.join(', ')})
998:           VALUES (${placeholders})
999:         `).run(...columns.map((column) => row[column]));
1000:       }
1001:     });
1002:     merge(backup_rows);
1003:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1004:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to re-merge checkpoint catalog during swap-done recovery (non-fatal): ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1005:   } finally {
1006:     if (backup_db) {
1007:       try { backup_db.close(); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1008:     }
1009:     if (live_db) {
1010:       try { live_db.close(); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1011:     }
1012:   }
1013: }
1014: 
1015: export function recover_interrupted_checkpoint_restore(target_path: string): boolean {
1016:   const journal_path = get_restore_journal_path(target_path);
1017:   if (!journal_path || !fs.existsSync(journal_path)) {
1018:     return false;
1019:   }
1020: 
1021:   const journal = read_restore_journal(journal_path);
1022:   if (!journal) {
1023:     return false;
1024:   }
1025: 
1026:   if (journal.phase === 'swap-done') {
1027:     // Re-merge any post-snapshot catalog rows from the .bak before deleting it; the
1028:     // crashed in-process restore would have done this merge had it not been interrupted.
1029:     merge_checkpoint_catalog_from_backup(journal.liveMainPath, journal.backupMainPath);
1030:     fs.rmSync(journal.backupMainPath, { force: true });
1031:     fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal.backupMainPath));
1032:     if (journal.shouldRestoreVec && journal.backupShardPath) {
1033:       fs.rmSync(journal.backupShardPath, { force: true });
1034:       fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal.backupShardPath));
1035:     }
1036:     write_needs_rebuild_sentinel_for_recovered_restore(journal.liveMainPath, journal.checkpointName);
1037:     fs.rmSync(journal_path, { force: true });
1038:     fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal_path));
1039:     console.warn('[vector-index] Finalized completed checkpoint restore from rollback journal');
1040:     return true;
1041:   }
1042: 
1043:   remove_sqlite_sidecars(journal.liveMainPath);
1044:   if (fs.existsSync(journal.backupMainPath)) {
1045:     fs.rmSync(journal.liveMainPath, { force: true });
1046:     fs.renameSync(journal.backupMainPath, journal.liveMainPath);
1047:     fsync_file_if_possible(journal.liveMainPath);
1048:     fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal.liveMainPath));
1049:   }
1050: 
1051:   if (journal.shouldRestoreVec && journal.liveShardPath) {
1052:     remove_sqlite_sidecars(journal.liveShardPath);
1053:     if (journal.liveShardPreexisted && journal.backupShardPath && fs.existsSync(journal.backupShardPath)) {
1054:       fs.rmSync(journal.liveShardPath, { force: true });
1055:       fs.renameSync(journal.backupShardPath, journal.liveShardPath);
1056:       fsync_file_if_possible(journal.liveShardPath);
1057:       fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal.liveShardPath));
1058:     } else if (!journal.liveShardPreexisted) {
1059:       fs.rmSync(journal.liveShardPath, { force: true });
1060:       fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal.liveShardPath));
1061:     }
1062:   }
1063: 
1064:   fs.rmSync(journal_path, { force: true });
1065:   fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(journal_path));
1066:   console.warn('[vector-index] Recovered interrupted checkpoint restore from rollback journal');
1067:   return true;
1068: }
1069: 
1070: export function migrate_active_legacy_profile_database(
1071:   canonicalPath: string,
1072:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
1073: ): void {
1074:   if (canonicalPath === ':memory:') {
1075:     return;
1076:   }
1077: 
1078:   const baseDir = path.dirname(canonicalPath);
1079:   const shardPath = get_vector_shard_path(profile, baseDir);
1080:   const legacyPath = legacy_profile_database_path(profile, baseDir);
1081:   if (path.resolve(legacyPath) === path.resolve(canonicalPath)) {
1082:     return;
1083:   }
1084: 
1085:   migrateLegacySingleDbToShardSync(legacyPath, canonicalPath, shardPath, profile);
1086: }

1088: export function attachActiveVectorShard(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
1089:   try {
1090:     sqliteVec.load(database);
1091:     sqlite_vec_available_flag = true;
1092:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1093:     sqlite_vec_available_flag = false;
1094:   }
1095:   database.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
1096:   const canonicalPath = get_database_file_path(database);
1097:   const baseDir = resolve_database_base_dir(database);
1098:   const shardPath = get_vector_shard_path(profile, baseDir);
1099:   const attachedPath = get_attached_vector_path(database);
1100: 
1101:   if (attachedPath && attachedPath !== path.resolve(shardPath)) {
1102:     drop_legacy_temp_aliases(database);
1103:     database.exec(`DETACH DATABASE ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}`);
1104:   } else if (attachedPath === path.resolve(shardPath)) {
1105:     ensure_vector_shard_schema(database, profile);
1106:     drop_canonical_vector_payload_tables(database);
1107:     create_legacy_temp_aliases(database, profile);
1108:     active_vector_source = {
1109:       canonical_path: canonicalPath,
1110:       shard_path: shardPath,
1111:       attached: true,
1112:       profile: {
1113:         provider: profile.provider,
1114:         model: profile.model,
1115:         dim: profile.dim,
1116:         dtype: profile.dtype,
1117:       },
1118:     };
1119:     return;
1120:   }
1121: 
1122:   database.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ${quote_sql_string(shardPath)} AS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}`);
1123:   ensure_vector_shard_schema(database, profile);
1124:   drop_canonical_vector_payload_tables(database);
1125:   create_legacy_temp_aliases(database, profile);
1126:   active_vector_source = {
1127:     canonical_path: canonicalPath,
1128:     shard_path: shardPath,
1129:     attached: true,
1130:     profile: {
1131:       provider: profile.provider,
1132:       model: profile.model,
1133:       dim: profile.dim,
1134:       dtype: profile.dtype,
1135:     },
1136:   };
1137: }
1138: 
1139: export function attachActiveVectorShardForActiveProfile(database: Database.Database): void {
1140:   attachActiveVectorShard(database, resolve_profile_for_active_embedder(database));
1141: }

1143: export function detachActiveVectorShard(database: Database.Database): void {

1200: const constitutional_cache = new Map<string, { data: MemoryRow[]; timestamp: number }>();
1201: const CONSTITUTIONAL_CACHE_TTL = 300000;
1202: const CONSTITUTIONAL_CACHE_MAX_KEYS = 50;

1205: const constitutional_cache_loading = new Map<string, boolean>();

1207: let last_db_mod_time = 0;
1208: let last_constitutional_cache_db_path: string | null = null;
1209: 
1210: function get_constitutional_cache_db_scope(): string {
1211:   if (db_path === ':memory:') {
1212:     return db_path;
1213:   }
1214: 
1215:   return path.resolve(db_path);
1216: }

1218: function build_constitutional_cache_key(
1219:   spec_folder: string | null,
1220:   includeArchived: boolean,
1221: ): string {
1222:   const db_scope = get_constitutional_cache_db_scope();
1223:   return `${db_scope}::${spec_folder || 'global'}:${includeArchived ? 'arch' : 'noarch'}`;
1224: }
1225: 
1226: function refresh_constitutional_cache_db_state(): void {
1227:   const current_db_path = get_constitutional_cache_db_scope();
1228:   last_constitutional_cache_db_path = current_db_path;
1229: 
1230:   if (current_db_path === ':memory:' || !fs.existsSync(current_db_path)) {
1231:     last_db_mod_time = 0;
1232:     return;
1233:   }
1234: 
1235:   last_db_mod_time = fs.statSync(current_db_path).mtimeMs;
1236: }
1237: 
1238: function is_constitutional_cache_valid() {
1239:   if (constitutional_cache.size === 0) return false;
1240: 
1241:   try {
1242:     const current_db_path = get_constitutional_cache_db_scope();
1243:     if (
1244:       last_constitutional_cache_db_path &&
1245:       last_constitutional_cache_db_path !== current_db_path
1246:     ) {
1247:       return false;
1248:     }
1249: 
1250:     if (current_db_path !== ':memory:' && fs.existsSync(current_db_path)) {
1251:       const stats = fs.statSync(current_db_path);
1252:       if (stats.mtimeMs > last_db_mod_time) {
1253:         last_db_mod_time = stats.mtimeMs;

1261:   return true;

1265:    4. PREPARED STATEMENT CACHING
1266: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/

1268: type PreparedStatements = {
1269:   count_all: Database.Statement<[], { count: number }>;
1270:   count_by_folder: Database.Statement<[string], { count: number }>;
1271:   get_by_id: Database.Statement<[number], MemoryRow | undefined>;
1272:   get_by_path: Database.Statement<[string], MemoryRow | undefined>;
1273:   get_by_folder_and_path: Database.Statement<[
1274:     string,
1275:     string,
1276:     string,
1277:     string | null,
1278:     string | null,
1279:     string | null,
1280:     string | null,
1281:     string | null,
1282:   ], { id: number } | undefined>;
1283:   get_stats: Database.Statement<[], { total: number; complete: number; pending: number; failed: number }>;
1284:   list_base: Database.Statement<[number, number], MemoryRow[]>;
1285: };
1286: // Scope prepared statements per Database instance via WeakMap.
1287: // The old global singleton would return stale statements from a prior DB connection
1288: // When called with a different database, executing queries against the wrong connection.
1289: const prepared_statements_cache = new WeakMap<Database.Database, PreparedStatements>();
1290: 
1291: /**
1292:  * Initializes cached prepared statements for common queries.
1293:  * @param database - The database connection to prepare against.
1294:  * @returns The prepared statements cache.
1295:  */
1296: export function init_prepared_statements(database: Database.Database): PreparedStatements {
1297:   const cached = prepared_statements_cache.get(database);
1298:   if (cached) return cached;
1299: 
1300:   const prepared_statements: PreparedStatements = {
1301:     count_all: database.prepare(`
1302:       SELECT COUNT(*) as count
1303:       FROM memory_index m
1304:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
1305:     `),
1306:     count_by_folder: database.prepare(`
1307:       SELECT COUNT(*) as count
1308:       FROM memory_index m
1309:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
1310:       WHERE m.spec_folder = ?
1311:     `),
1312:     get_by_id: database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?'),
1313:     get_by_path: database.prepare(`
1314:       SELECT m.*
1315:       FROM memory_index m
1316:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
1317:       WHERE m.file_path = ?
1318:     `),
1319:     get_by_folder_and_path: database.prepare(`
1320:       SELECT m.id
1321:       FROM memory_index m
1322:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
1323:       WHERE m.spec_folder = ?
1324:         AND (m.canonical_file_path = ? OR m.file_path = ?)
1325:         AND COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(m.anchor_id), ''), '_') = COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(?), ''), '_')
1326:         AND COALESCE(m.tenant_id,'') = COALESCE(?, '')
1327:         AND COALESCE(m.user_id,'') = COALESCE(?, '')
1328:         AND COALESCE(m.agent_id,'') = COALESCE(?, '')
1329:         AND COALESCE(m.session_id,'') = COALESCE(?, '')
1330:       ORDER BY m.id DESC
1331:       LIMIT 1
1332:     `),
1333:     get_stats: database.prepare(`
1334:       SELECT
1335:         COUNT(*) as total,
1336:         SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as complete,
1337:         SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as pending,
1338:         SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed
1339:       FROM memory_index m
1340:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
1341:     `),
1342:     list_base: database.prepare(`
1343:       SELECT m.*
1344:       FROM memory_index m
1345:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
1346:       ORDER BY m.created_at DESC
1347:       LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
1348:     `)
1349:   };
1350: 
1351:   prepared_statements_cache.set(database, prepared_statements);
1352:   return prepared_statements;
1353: }
1354: 
1355: /**
1356:  * Clears cached prepared statements for a specific database or all databases.
1357:  * @param database - Optional: clear only for this database. If omitted, the
1358:  *   WeakMap self-cleans when the Database object is GC'd, so this is a no-op.
1359:  * @returns Nothing.
1360:  */
1361: export function clear_prepared_statements(database?: Database.Database): void {
1362:   if (database) {

1364:   }
1365:   // WeakMap entries are automatically cleared when the Database key is GC'd.
1366: }

247: function hasTable(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
248:   const row = database.prepare(
249:     "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name = ?"
250:   ).get(tableName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
251: 
252:   return row?.present === 1;
253: }
254: 
255: function hasIndex(database: Database.Database, indexName: string): boolean {
256:   const row = database.prepare(
257:     "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name = ?"
258:   ).get(indexName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
259: 
260:   return row?.present === 1;
261: }

263: function getTableColumns(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string[] {
264:   return (database.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
265:     .map((column) => column.name)
266:     .filter((columnName) => typeof columnName === 'string' && columnName.length > 0);
267: }

269: function getTableSql(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string | null {
270:   const row = database.prepare(
271:     "SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name = ?"
272:   ).get(tableName) as { sql?: string } | undefined;
273: 
274:   return typeof row?.sql === 'string' ? row.sql : null;
275: }

278:   const tableSql = getTableSql(database, 'embedding_cache');

281:   }
282: 
283:   return /PRIMARY\s+KEY\s*\(\s*content_hash\s*,\s*model_id\s*,\s*dimensions\s*\)/i.test(tableSql)
284:     || /PRIMARY\s+KEY\s*\(\s*content_hash\s*,\s*profile_key\s*,\s*input_kind\s*,\s*model_id\s*,\s*dimensions\s*\)/i.test(tableSql);
285: }

328: function createRequiredIndex(
329:   database: Database.Database,
330:   indexName: string,
331:   sql: string,
332:   context: string,
333: ): void {
334:   try {
335:     database.exec(sql);
336:   } catch (error: unknown) {
337:     if (hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
338:       logger.warn(`${context}: reusing existing index after DDL warning`, {
339:         indexName,
340:         error: get_error_message(error),
341:       });
342:       return;
343:     }
344:     throw error;
345:   }
346: 
347:   if (!hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
348:     throw new Error(`${context}: expected index "${indexName}" to exist after migration`);
349:   }
350: }

392: function getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(
393:   availableColumns: Set<string>,
394:   candidates: string[],
395:   fallback: string,
396: ): string {
397:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
398:     if (availableColumns.has(candidate)) {
399:       return candidate;
400:     }
401:   }
402:   return fallback;
403: }

414: function migrateMemoryConflictsTable(database: Database.Database): void {
415:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
416:     createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
417:     return;
418:   }
419: 
420:   if (hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database)) {
421:     return;
422:   }
423: 
424:   database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_conflicts RENAME TO memory_conflicts_legacy');
425:   createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
426: 
427:   const legacyColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_conflicts_legacy'));
428:   database.exec(`
429:     INSERT INTO memory_conflicts (
430:       id,
431:       timestamp,
432:       action,
433:       new_memory_hash,
434:       new_memory_id,
435:       existing_memory_id,
436:       similarity,
437:       reason,
438:       new_content_preview,
439:       existing_content_preview,
440:       contradiction_detected,
441:       contradiction_type,
442:       spec_folder,
443:       created_at
444:     )
445:     SELECT
446:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['id'], 'NULL')},
447:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['timestamp', 'created_at'], 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP')},
448:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['action'], 'NULL')},
449:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_memory_hash'], 'NULL')},
450:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_memory_id'], 'NULL')},
451:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['existing_memory_id'], 'NULL')},
452:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['similarity', 'similarity_score'], 'NULL')},
453:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['reason', 'notes'], 'NULL')},
454:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_content_preview'], 'NULL')},
455:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['existing_content_preview'], 'NULL')},
456:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['contradiction_detected'], '0')},
457:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['contradiction_type'], 'NULL')},
458:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['spec_folder'], 'NULL')},
459:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['created_at', 'timestamp'], 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP')}
460:     FROM memory_conflicts_legacy
461:   `);
462:   database.exec('DROP TABLE memory_conflicts_legacy');
463: 
464:   if (!hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database)) {
465:     throw new Error('Migration v12: memory_conflicts table is still missing unified columns after rebuild');
466:   }
467: }

469: function summarizeCompatibilityFailures(report: SchemaCompatibilityReport): string {
470:   const details: string[] = [];
471: 
472:   if (report.missingTables.length > 0) {
473:     details.push(`missing tables: ${report.missingTables.join(', ')}`);
474:   }
475:   if (Object.keys(report.missingColumns).length > 0) {
476:     const missingColumns = Object.entries(report.missingColumns)
477:       .map(([tableName, columns]) => `${tableName}[${columns.join(', ')}]`)
478:       .join('; ');
479:     details.push(`missing columns: ${missingColumns}`);
480:   }
481:   if (report.missingIndexes.length > 0) {
482:     details.push(`missing indexes: ${report.missingIndexes.join(', ')}`);
483:   }
484:   if (report.constraintMismatches.length > 0) {
485:     details.push(`constraint mismatches: ${report.constraintMismatches.join(', ')}`);
486:   }
487: 
488:   return details.join(' | ');
489: }

725: function sourceKindFromProvenanceSource(value: unknown): 'human' | 'agent' | 'system' | 'import' | 'feedback' {
726:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0) {
727:     return 'system';
728:   }
729: 
730:   const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
731:   if (/\b(human|manual|user|operator|author)\b/.test(normalized)) {
732:     return 'human';
733:   }
734:   if (normalized.includes('feedback') || normalized.includes('validate') || normalized.includes('validation')) {
735:     return 'feedback';
736:   }
737:   if (normalized.includes('import') || normalized.includes('ingest') || normalized.includes('scan') || normalized.includes('index')) {
738:     return 'import';
739:   }
740:   if (/\b(agent|assistant|claude|codex|opencode|automation|bot)\b/.test(normalized)) {
741:     return 'agent';
742:   }
743:   return 'system';
744: }

746: function ensureMemoryIndexSourceKindColumn(database: Database.Database, context: string): void {
747:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
748:     logger.warn(`${context}: memory_index missing; skipping source_kind column`);
749:     return;
750:   }
751: 
752:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index'));
753:   if (!columns.has('source_kind')) {
754:     try {
755:       database.exec(SOURCE_KIND_COLUMN_SQL);
756:       columns.add('source_kind');
757:       logger.info(`${context}: Added memory_index.source_kind`);
758:     } catch (error: unknown) {
759:       if (!get_error_message(error).includes('duplicate column')) {
760:         throw error;
761:       }
762:       logDuplicateColumnMigrationSkip('source_kind', error);
763:     }
764:   }
765: 
766:   if (columns.has('provenance_source')) {
767:     const rows = database.prepare(`
768:       SELECT id, provenance_source
769:       FROM memory_index
770:       WHERE source_kind IS NULL OR TRIM(source_kind) = ''
771:         OR source_kind NOT IN ('human', 'agent', 'system', 'import', 'feedback')
772:         OR (source_kind = 'system' AND provenance_source IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(provenance_source) != '')
773:     `).all() as Array<{ id: number; provenance_source: string | null }>;
774:     const update = database.prepare('UPDATE memory_index SET source_kind = ? WHERE id = ?');
775:     for (const row of rows) {
776:       update.run(sourceKindFromProvenanceSource(row.provenance_source), row.id);
777:     }
778:   } else {
779:     database.prepare(`
780:       UPDATE memory_index
781:       SET source_kind = 'system'
782:       WHERE source_kind IS NULL OR TRIM(source_kind) = ''
783:         OR source_kind NOT IN ('human', 'agent', 'system', 'import', 'feedback')
784:     `).run();
785:   }
786: }

838: /**
839:  * Deprecate-before-create pre-pass for the active-row logical-key unique index.
840:  *
841:  * A pre-existing database can already hold duplicate active rows for one logical key
842:  * (e.g. created before the supersede-on-reindex guard existed). Creating the UNIQUE
843:  * index over those rows throws, and since all migrations share one transaction, the
844:  * whole upgrade batch rolls back and the schema wedges. To advance safely we retire the
845:  * duplicates first — deprecate, never delete — by setting the losers' importance_tier to
846:  * 'deprecated' (the same retirement the runtime reindex guard uses), which removes them
847:  * from the partial index predicate so the UNIQUE index can be built. Within each duplicate
848:  * group the winner is the highest importance tier, then the most recently updated row.
849:  *
850:  * Grouping mirrors the index exactly. Tolerant when a scope/anchor column is absent on an
851:  * older schema (the group key falls back to the empty string for the missing column, the
852:  * same as the index's COALESCE), and a no-op when the table or needed columns are missing.
853:  */
854: function deprecateDuplicateActiveLogicalKeysBeforeUniqueIndex(database: Database.Database): void {
855:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
856:     return;
857:   }
858: 
859:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index'));
860:   // canonical_file_path / file_path and importance_tier are required to form the key and
861:   // pick a winner; without them this DB predates the guarded schema and there is nothing
862:   // to reconcile here.
863:   if (!columns.has('importance_tier') || (!columns.has('canonical_file_path') && !columns.has('file_path'))) {
864:     return;
865:   }
866: 
867:   // Build per-column key fragments that match the index's COALESCE expressions, degrading
868:   // to a literal '' for columns this older schema does not have (so the key shape is stable).
869:   const pathExpr = columns.has('canonical_file_path')
870:     ? "COALESCE(NULLIF(canonical_file_path, ''), file_path)"
871:     : 'file_path';
872:   const anchorExpr = columns.has('anchor_id')
873:     ? "COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(anchor_id), ''), '_')"
874:     : "'_'";
875:   const scopeExpr = (col: string): string => (columns.has(col) ? `COALESCE(${col}, '')` : "''");
876: 
877:   const keyExpr = [
878:     'spec_folder',
879:     pathExpr,
880:     anchorExpr,
881:     scopeExpr('tenant_id'),
882:     scopeExpr('user_id'),
883:     scopeExpr('agent_id'),
884:     scopeExpr('session_id'),

887:   // Active = the same predicate the unique index uses (excludes constitutional + deprecated).
888:   const activePredicate = "COALESCE(importance_tier, 'normal') NOT IN ('constitutional', 'deprecated')";

890:   // Tier rank mirrors IMPORTANCE_TIERS values so the highest-priority active row wins.
891:   const tierRankExpr = `CASE COALESCE(importance_tier, 'normal')
892:         WHEN 'critical' THEN 5
893:         WHEN 'important' THEN 4
894:         WHEN 'normal' THEN 3
895:         WHEN 'temporary' THEN 2
896:         ELSE 1
897:       END`;
898: 
899:   // Find groups with more than one active row, then deprecate every row except the winner
900:   // (highest tier rank, then most recently updated, then highest id as a stable tiebreak).
901:   const retireStmt = database.prepare(`
902:     UPDATE memory_index
903:     SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
904:         updated_at = datetime('now')
905:     WHERE id IN (
906:       SELECT id FROM (
907:         SELECT id,
908:                (${keyExpr}) AS logical_key,
909:                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
910:                  PARTITION BY (${keyExpr})
911:                  ORDER BY ${tierRankExpr} DESC, updated_at DESC, id DESC
912:                ) AS rn
913:         FROM memory_index
914:         WHERE ${activePredicate}
915:       )
916:       WHERE rn > 1
917:     )
918:   `);

920:   const result = retireStmt.run() as { changes: number };

923:       `Migration v28: deprecated ${result.changes} duplicate active row(s) before building idx_memory_logical_key_active_unique`,

36:   init_prepared_statements,
37:   validate_file_path_local,
38:   safe_read_file_async,
39:   safe_parse_json,
40:   search_weights,
41:   sqlite_vec_available as get_sqlite_vec_available,
42:   activeVectorSource as activeVectorSchema,
43: } from './vector-index-store.js';
44: import { delete_memory_from_database } from './vector-index-mutations.js';
45: import type {
46:   EmbeddingInput,
47:   EnrichedSearchResult,
48:   MemoryRow,
49:   VectorSearchOptions,
50: } from './vector-index-types.js';
51: 
52: const logger = createLogger('VectorIndex');
53: 
54: type RelatedMemoryLink = { id: number; similarity: number };
55: type UsageStatsOptions = { sortBy?: string; order?: string; limit?: number };
56: type CleanupOptions = {
57:   maxAgeDays?: number;
58:   maxAccessCount?: number;
59:   maxConfidence?: number;
60:   limit?: number;
61: };
62: type VectorSource = {
63:   dim: number;
64:   tableName: string;
65:   idColumn: string;
66:   embeddingColumn: string;
67: };
68: 
69: function appendSpecFolderScope(
70:   clauses: string[],
71:   params: unknown[],
72:   specFolder: string | null | undefined,
73:   column = 'm.spec_folder',
74: ): void {
75:   if (!specFolder) return;
76:   clauses.push(`(${column} = ? OR ${column} LIKE ?)`);
77:   params.push(specFolder, `${specFolder}/%`);
78: }
79: 
80: function tableExists(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
81:   const row = database.prepare(`
82:     SELECT 1 AS found
83:     FROM active_vec.sqlite_master
84:     WHERE type = 'table' AND name = ?
85:     LIMIT 1
86:   `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
87:   return row?.found === 1;
88: }
89: 
90: function getActiveVectorSourceForQuery(database: Database.Database): VectorSource {
91:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
92:   const dimTableName = `vec_${active.dim}`;
93:   if (active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && tableExists(database, dimTableName)) {
94:     return {
95:       dim: active.dim,
96:       tableName: activeVectorSchema(dimTableName),
97:       idColumn: 'id',
98:       embeddingColumn: 'vec',
99:     };
100:   }
101: 
102:   return {
103:     dim: get_embedding_dim(),
104:     tableName: activeVectorSchema('vec_memories'),
105:     idColumn: 'rowid',
106:     embeddingColumn: 'embedding',
107:   };
108: }

114: /**
115:  * Gets one indexed memory by identifier.
116:  * @param id - The memory identifier.
117:  * @returns The matching memory row, if found.
118:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates store initialization failures from the vector index.
119:  * @example
120:  * ```ts
121:  * const memory = get_memory(42);
122:  * ```
123:  */
124: export function get_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): MemoryRow | null {
125:   const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
126:   const row = stmts.get_by_id.get(id);
127: 
128:   if (row) {
129:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
130:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
131:   }
132: 
133:   return row || null;
134: }

136: /**
137:  * Gets indexed memories for a spec folder.
138:  * @param spec_folder - The spec folder to query.
139:  * @returns The memory rows for the folder.
140:  */
141: export function get_memories_by_folder(
142:   spec_folder: string,
143:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
144: ): MemoryRow[] {
145:   const rows = database.prepare(`
146:     SELECT m.*
147:     FROM memory_index m
148:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
149:     WHERE (m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ?)
150:     ORDER BY m.created_at DESC
151:   `).all(spec_folder, `${spec_folder}/%`) as MemoryRow[];
152: 
153:   return rows.map((row: MemoryRow) => {
154:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
155:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
156:     return row;
157:   });
158: }

160: /**
161:  * Gets the total number of indexed memories.
162:  * @returns The total memory count.
163:  */
164: export function get_memory_count(database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): number {
165:   const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
166:   const result = stmts.count_all.get();
167:   return result?.count ?? 0;
168: }
169: 
170: /**
171:  * Gets memory counts grouped by embedding status.
172:  * @returns The counts for each embedding status.
173:  */
174: export function get_status_counts(
175:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
176: ): { pending: number; success: number; failed: number; retry: number; partial: number } {
177:   const rows = database.prepare(`
178:     SELECT m.embedding_status, COUNT(*) as count
179:     FROM memory_index m
180:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
181:     GROUP BY m.embedding_status
182:   `).all();
183: 
184:   // Include 'partial' status in counts.
185:   const counts = { pending: 0, success: 0, failed: 0, retry: 0, partial: 0 };
186:   for (const row of rows as Array<{ embedding_status: keyof typeof counts; count: number }>) {
187:     if (row.embedding_status in counts) {
188:       counts[row.embedding_status] = row.count;
189:     }
190:   }
191: 
192:   return counts;
193: }
194: 
195: /**
196:  * Gets summary counts for indexed memories.
197:  * @returns The aggregate memory statistics.
198:  */
199: export function get_stats(
200:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
201: ): { total: number; pending: number; success: number; failed: number; retry: number; partial: number } {
202:   const counts = get_status_counts(database);
203:   const total = counts.pending + counts.success + counts.failed + counts.retry + counts.partial;
204: 
205:   return {
206:     total,
207:     ...counts
208:   };
209: }
