387: // 8. BM25 SEARCH
388: 
389: /**
390:  * Search the BM25 index with optional spec folder filtering.
391:  * @param query - The search query string.
392:  * @param options - Optional limit and specFolder filter.
393:  * @returns Array of BM25-scored results tagged with source 'bm25'.
394:  */
395: function bm25Search(
396:   query: string,
397:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string } = {}
398: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
399:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
400:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] BM25 not enabled — returning empty bm25Search results');
401:     return [];
402:   }
403: 
404:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder } = options;
405: 
406:   try {
407:     if (shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine(db)) {
408:       return ftsSearch(query, {
409:         limit,
410:         specFolder,
411:       }).map((result) => ({
412:         ...result,
413:         bm25_score: result.score,
414:         source: 'bm25',
415:         sources: Array.from(new Set([
416:           ...(
417:             Array.isArray((result as Record<string, unknown>).sources)
418:               ? ((result as Record<string, unknown>).sources as unknown[]).filter((source): source is string => typeof source === 'string')
419:               : []
420:           ),
421:           'fts',
422:           'bm25',
423:         ])),
424:       }));
425:     }
426: 
427:     const index = getIndex();
428:     const results = index.search(query, limit);
429: 
430:     // BM25 document IDs are stringified
431:     // Numeric memory IDs (e.g., "42"), not spec folder paths. The old filter compared
432:     // R.id against specFolder which never matched. Use DB lookup to resolve spec_folder.
433: 
434:     // Batch-resolve metadata for all result IDs (was N+1 individual queries)
435:     // SECURITY: Spec-folder scope MUST fail closed — any error in scope
436:     // resolution returns [] rather than leaking unscoped BM25 candidates.
437:     let memoryMetadataMap: Map<number, Bm25MemoryMetadata> | null = null;
438:     if (results.length > 0) {
439:       if (!db) {
440:         if (specFolder) {
441:           const error = new Error('Database unavailable for spec-folder scope lookup');
442:           console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
443:           return [];
444:         }
445:       } else {
446:         try {
447:           const ids = results.map((r: { id: string }) => Number(r.id));
448:           const placeholders = ids.map(() => '?').join(',');
449:           const rows = db.prepare(
450:             `SELECT id, spec_folder, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`
451:           ).all(...ids) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null; importance_tier: string | null }>;
452:           memoryMetadataMap = new Map();
453:           for (const row of rows) {
454:             memoryMetadataMap.set(row.id, {
455:               specFolder: row.spec_folder,
456:               importanceTier: row.importance_tier,
457:             });
458:           }
459:         } catch (error: unknown) {
460:           console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
461:           return [];
462:         }
463:       }
464: 
465:       // DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: If specFolder was requested but metadata resolution
466:       // is still null after the resolution block, something unexpected happened.
467:       // Fail closed rather than falling through to unscoped results.
468:       if (specFolder && !memoryMetadataMap) {
469:         const error = new Error('memoryMetadataMap unexpectedly null after scope resolution');
470:         console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
471:         return [];
472:       }
473:     }
474: 
475:     return results
476:       .filter((r: { id: string }) => {
477:         const metadata = memoryMetadataMap?.get(Number(r.id));
478:         if (metadata?.importanceTier === 'deprecated') return false;
479:         if (!specFolder) return true;
480:         if (!metadata) return false;
481:         const folder = metadata.specFolder;
482:         if (!folder) return false;
483:         return folder === specFolder || folder.startsWith(specFolder + '/');
484:       })
485:       .map((r: { id: string; score: number }) => ({
486:         ...r,
487:         source: 'bm25',
488:       }));
489:   } catch (error: unknown) {
490:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
491:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] BM25 search failed: ${msg}`);
492:     return [];
493:   }
494: }
495: 
496: /**
497:  * Check whether the BM25 index is populated and available for search.
498:  * @returns True if the BM25 index exists and contains at least one document.
499:  */
500: function isBm25Available(): boolean {
501:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
502:     return false;
503:   }
504: 
505:   try {
506:     const index = getIndex();
507:     return index.getStats().documentCount > 0;
508:   } catch (_err: unknown) {
509:     // Swallow index-not-initialized errors; caller treats absence as unavailable
510:     return false;
511:   }
512: }
513: 
514: // 9. FTS SEARCH
515: 
516: /**
517:  * Check whether the FTS5 full-text search table exists in the database.
518:  * @returns True if the memory_fts table exists in the connected database.
519:  */
520: function isFtsAvailable(): boolean {
521:   if (!db) {
522:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] db not initialized — isFtsAvailable returning false');
523:     return false;
524:   }
525: 
526:   try {
527:     const result = (db.prepare(`
528:       SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
529:     `) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
530:     return !!result;
531:   } catch (_err: unknown) {
532:     // Swallow DB errors; caller treats absence as unavailable
533:     return false;
534:   }
535: }
536: 
537: /**
538:  * Run FTS5 full-text search with weighted BM25 scoring and optional spec folder filtering.
539:  * @param query - The search query string.
540:  * @param options - Optional limit, specFolder filter, and includeArchived flag.
541:  * @returns Array of FTS-scored results tagged with source 'fts'.
542:  */
543: function ftsSearch(
544:   query: string,
545:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
546: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
547:   if (!db || !isFtsAvailable()) {
548:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] db not initialized or FTS unavailable — returning empty ftsSearch results');
549:     return [];
550:   }
551: 
552:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder, includeArchived = false } = options;
553: 
554:   try {
555:     // Delegate to weighted BM25 FTS5 search from sqlite-fts.ts
556:     // Uses bm25(memory_fts, 10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0) for per-column weighting
557:     // (title 10x, trigger_phrases 5x, file_path 2x, content 1x)
558:     // Filters: deprecated-tier exclusion and spec-folder matching handled by fts5Bm25Search
559:     const bm25Results = fts5Bm25Search(db, query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
560: 
561:     return bm25Results.map(row => ({
562:       ...row,
563:       id: row.id as number,
564:       score: row.fts_score || 0,
565:       source: 'fts',
566:     }));
567:   } catch (error: unknown) {
568:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
569:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] FTS search failed: ${msg}`);
570:     return [];
571:   }
572: }
573: 
574: // 10. COMBINED LEXICAL SEARCH
575: 
576: /**
577:  * Merge FTS and BM25 search results, deduplicating by ID and preferring FTS scores.
578:  * @param query - The search query string.
579:  * @param options - Optional limit, specFolder filter, and includeArchived flag.
580:  * @returns Deduplicated array of merged results sorted by score descending.
581:  */
582: function combinedLexicalSearch(
583:   query: string,
584:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
585: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
586:   const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, options);
587:   const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, options);
588: 
589:   // Merge by canonical ID, prefer FTS scores.
590:   // CanonicalResultId() prevents duplicate rows when one channel emits
591:   // Numeric IDs (42) and another emits string IDs ("42" or "mem:42").
592:   const merged = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
593: 
594:   for (const r of ftsResults) {
595:     merged.set(canonicalResultId(r.id), r);
596:   }
597: 
598:   for (const r of bm25Results) {
599:     const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
600:     if (!merged.has(canonicalId)) {
601:       merged.set(canonicalId, r);
602:     }
603:   }
604: 
605:   return Array.from(merged.values())
606:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
607:     .slice(0, options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT);
608: }
609: 
610: function normalizeTriggerText(value: string): string {
611:   return value
612:     .toLowerCase()
613:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, ' ')
614:     .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
615:     .trim();
616: }
617: 
618: function triggerQueryTokens(query: string): string[] {
619:   return Array.from(new Set(
620:     normalizeTriggerText(query)
621:       .split(/\s+/)
622:       .filter((token) => token.length >= 2)
623:   ));
624: }
625: 
626: function computeTriggerMatchScore(query: string, phrase: string): number {
627:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeTriggerText(query);
628:   const normalizedPhrase = normalizeTriggerText(phrase);
629:   if (!normalizedQuery || !normalizedPhrase) return 0;
630:   if (normalizedPhrase === normalizedQuery) return 1.0;
631: 
632:   const queryTokens = triggerQueryTokens(normalizedQuery);
633:   const phraseTokens = new Set(triggerQueryTokens(normalizedPhrase));
634:   if (phraseTokens.size < 2) return 0;
635:   if (normalizedPhrase.includes(normalizedQuery)) return 0.94;
636:   if (normalizedQuery.includes(normalizedPhrase)) return 0.88;
637:   if (queryTokens.length === 0) return 0;
638:   const overlap = queryTokens.filter((token) => phraseTokens.has(token)).length;
639:   const coverage = overlap / queryTokens.length;
640:   return coverage >= 0.8 ? coverage * 0.75 : 0;
641: }
642: 
643: function timestampBoost(value: unknown): number {
644:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.length === 0) return 0;
645:   const parsed = Date.parse(value);
646:   if (!Number.isFinite(parsed)) return 0;
647:   const ageDays = Math.max(0, (Date.now() - parsed) / 86_400_000);
648:   return 1 / (1 + ageDays / 30);
649: }
650: 
651: function exactTriggerSearch(
652:   query: string,
653:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
654: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
655:   if (!db) return [];
656: 
657:   const tokens = triggerQueryTokens(query).filter((token) => token.length >= 3).slice(0, 8);
658:   if (tokens.length === 0) return [];
659: 
660:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder } = options;
661:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeTriggerText(query);
662:   const exactPhraseLike = `%${normalizedQuery}%`;
663:   const candidateLimit = Math.max(200, Math.min(1000, limit * 100));
664:   const conditions = [
665:     `m.trigger_phrases IS NOT NULL`,
666:     `m.trigger_phrases != ''`,
667:     `m.trigger_phrases != '[]'`,
668:     `(m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != 'deprecated')`,
669:     `(m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > datetime('now'))`,
670:     `(${tokens.map(() => 'LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ?').join(' OR ')})`,
671:   ];
672:   const params: unknown[] = tokens.map((token) => `%${token}%`);
673: 
674:   if (specFolder) {
675:     conditions.push(`(m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ?)`);
676:     params.push(specFolder, `${specFolder}/%`);
677:   }
678: 
679:   try {
680:     const rows = db.prepare(`
681:       SELECT m.*
682:       FROM memory_index m
683:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
684:       WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}
685:       ORDER BY
686:         CASE WHEN LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ? THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
687:         COALESCE(m.updated_at, m.created_at, '') DESC
688:       LIMIT ${candidateLimit}
689:     `).all(...params, exactPhraseLike) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
690: 
691:     return rows
692:       .map((row): HybridSearchResult | null => {
693:         const phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases as string | string[] | null | undefined);
694:         const matchScore = Math.max(0, ...phrases.map((phrase) => computeTriggerMatchScore(query, phrase)));
695:         if (matchScore <= 0) return null;
696: 
697:         const importance = typeof row.importance_weight === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.importance_weight)
698:           ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, row.importance_weight))
699:           : 0.5;
700:         const recency = timestampBoost(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);
701:         const score = Math.min(1, matchScore + importance * 0.03 + recency * 0.04);
702: 
703:         return {
704:           ...row,
705:           id: row.id as number,
706:           score,
707:           triggerScore: matchScore,
708:           source: 'trigger',
709:         };
710:       })
711:       .filter((row): row is HybridSearchResult => row !== null)
712:       .sort((a, b) => {
713:         const scoreDelta = b.score - a.score;
714:         if (scoreDelta !== 0) return scoreDelta;
715:         const bTime = Date.parse(String(b.updated_at ?? b.created_at ?? '')) || 0;
716:         const aTime = Date.parse(String(a.updated_at ?? a.created_at ?? '')) || 0;
717:         return bTime - aTime;
718:       })
719:       .slice(0, limit);
720:   } catch (error: unknown) {
721:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
722:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Trigger phrase search failed: ${msg}`);
723:     return [];
724:   }
725: }
726: 
727: type RawChannelList = {
728:   source: string;
729:   results: Array<{ id: number | string; [key: string]: unknown }>;
730: };
731: 
732: function resolveRawCandidateScore(result: { score?: unknown; similarity?: unknown }): number {
733:   if (typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)) {
734:     return result.score;
735:   }
736:   if (typeof result.similarity === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.similarity)) {
737:     return result.similarity / 100;
738:   }
739:   return 0;
740: }
741: 
742: function getCandidateSources(result: HybridSearchResult): string[] {
743:   const sourceList = (result as { sources?: unknown }).sources;
744:   if (Array.isArray(sourceList)) {
745:     return sourceList.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
746:   }
747:   return typeof result.source === 'string' && result.source.length > 0
748:     ? [result.source]
749:     : [];
750: }
751: 
752: function getCandidateSourceScores(result: HybridSearchResult): Record<string, number> {
753:   const sourceScores = (result as { sourceScores?: unknown }).sourceScores;
754:   if (sourceScores && typeof sourceScores === 'object' && !Array.isArray(sourceScores)) {
755:     const normalizedScores: Record<string, number> = {};
756:     for (const [source, score] of Object.entries(sourceScores as Record<string, unknown>)) {
757:       if (typeof score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(score)) {
758:         normalizedScores[source] = score;
759:       }
760:     }
761:     return normalizedScores;
762:   }
763: 
764:   const fallbackScores: Record<string, number> = {};
765:   const resultScore = typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)
766:     ? result.score
767:     : 0;
768:   for (const source of getCandidateSources(result)) {
769:     fallbackScores[source] = resultScore;
770:   }
771:   return fallbackScores;
772: }
773: 
774: function mergeRawCandidate(
775:   existing: HybridSearchResult | undefined,
776:   incoming: HybridSearchResult
777: ): HybridSearchResult {
778:   if (!existing) {
779:     const sources = getCandidateSources(incoming);
780:     const channelCount = sources.length;
781:     return {
782:       ...incoming,
783:       sources,
784:       channelCount,
785:       sourceScores: getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
786:       channelAttribution: sources,
787:     };
788:   }
789: 
790:   const existingScore = typeof existing.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(existing.score)
791:     ? existing.score
792:     : 0;
793:   const incomingScore = typeof incoming.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(incoming.score)
794:     ? incoming.score
795:     : 0;
796:   const primary = incomingScore > existingScore ? incoming : existing;
797:   const secondary = primary === incoming ? existing : incoming;
798:   const sources = Array.from(new Set([
799:     ...getCandidateSources(existing),
800:     ...getCandidateSources(incoming),
801:   ]));
802:   const channelCount = sources.length;
803:   const crossChannelBonus = Math.min(0.06, 0.02 * Math.max(0, channelCount - 1));
804:   const mergedScore = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Math.max(existingScore, incomingScore) + crossChannelBonus));
805: 
806:   return {
807:     ...(secondary ?? {}),
808:     ...primary,
809:     score: mergedScore,
810:     source: typeof primary.source === 'string' ? primary.source : (sources[0] ?? 'hybrid'),
811:     sources,
812:     channelCount,
813:     sourceScores: {
814:       ...getCandidateSourceScores(existing),
815:       ...getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
816:     },
817:     channelAttribution: sources,
818:   };
819: }
820: 
821: function collectCandidatesFromLists(
822:   lists: RawChannelList[],
823:   limit?: number
824: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
825:   const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
826: 
827:   for (const list of lists) {
828:     if (!Array.isArray(list.results) || list.results.length === 0) continue;
829: 
830:     const scored = list.results.map((result) => {
831:       const rawScore = resolveRawCandidateScore(result as { score?: unknown; similarity?: unknown });
832:       return { result, rawScore };
833:     });
834: 
835:     let min = Infinity;
836:     let max = -Infinity;
837:     for (const { rawScore } of scored) {
838:       if (rawScore < min) min = rawScore;
839:       if (rawScore > max) max = rawScore;
840:     }
841:     const range = max - min;
842: 
843:     for (const { result, rawScore } of scored) {
844:       const normalizedScore = range > 0 ? (rawScore - min) / range : (rawScore > 0 ? 1.0 : 0);
845:       const candidate: HybridSearchResult = {
846:         ...result,
847:         id: result.id,
848:         source: list.source,
849:         score: normalizedScore,
850:         sources: [list.source],
851:         sourceScores: { [list.source]: normalizedScore },
852:         channelAttribution: [list.source],
853:       };
854:       const key = canonicalResultId(candidate.id);
855:       deduped.set(key, mergeRawCandidate(deduped.get(key), candidate));
856:     }
857:   }
858: 
859:   return applyResultLimit(
860:     Array.from(deduped.values()).sort((a, b) => {
861:       const aScore = typeof a.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(a.score) ? a.score : 0;
862:       const bScore = typeof b.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(b.score) ? b.score : 0;
863:       return bScore - aScore;
864:     }),
865:     limit
866:   );
867: }
868: 
869: function mergeRawCandidateSets(
870:   existing: HybridSearchResult[],
871:   incoming: HybridSearchResult[],
872:   limit?: number
873: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
874:   const merged = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
875: 
876:   for (const result of [...existing, ...incoming]) {
877:     const key = canonicalResultId(result.id);
878:     merged.set(key, mergeRawCandidate(merged.get(key), result));
879:   }
880: 
881:   return applyResultLimit(
882:     Array.from(merged.values()).sort((a, b) => {
883:       const aScore = typeof a.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(a.score) ? a.score : 0;
884:       const bScore = typeof b.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(b.score) ? b.score : 0;
885:       return bScore - aScore;
886:     }),
887:     limit
888:   );
889: }
890: 
891: function getAllowedChannels(options: HybridSearchOptions): Set<ChannelName> {
892:   const allowed = new Set<ChannelName>(['vector', 'fts', 'graph', 'degree']);
893: 
894:   if (isBm25Enabled()) {
895:     allowed.add('bm25');
896:   }
897: 
898:   if (options.useVector === false) allowed.delete('vector');
899:   if (options.useBm25 === false) allowed.delete('bm25');
900:   if (options.useFts === false) allowed.delete('fts');
901:   if (options.useGraph === false) {
902:     allowed.delete('graph');
903:     allowed.delete('degree');
904:   }
905: 
906:   return allowed;
907: }
908: 
909: function applyAllowedChannelOverrides(
910:   options: HybridSearchOptions,
911:   allowedChannels: Set<ChannelName>,
912:   overrides: Partial<HybridSearchOptions> = {}
913: ): HybridSearchOptions {
914:   return {
915:     ...options,
916:     ...overrides,
917:     useVector: allowedChannels.has('vector'),
918:     useBm25: allowedChannels.has('bm25'),
919:     useFts: allowedChannels.has('fts'),
920:     useGraph: allowedChannels.has('graph'),
921:   };
922: }
923: 
924: type FallbackPlanKind = 'adaptive' | 'tiered';
925: type FallbackStageName = 'primary' | 'retry';
926: 
927: interface FallbackPlanStage {
928:   stage: FallbackStageName;
929:   options: HybridSearchOptions;
930:   results: HybridSearchResult[];
931:   execution: HybridFusionExecution | null;
932:   trigger?: 'empty' | DegradationTrigger;
933: }
934: 
935: interface FallbackPlanExecution {
936:   allowedChannels: Set<ChannelName>;
937:   stages: FallbackPlanStage[];
938: }
939: 
940: interface HybridFusionExecution {
941:   evaluationMode: boolean;
942:   intent: string;
943:   lists: Array<{
944:     source: string;
945:     results: Array<{ id: number | string; [key: string]: unknown }>;
946:     weight?: number;
947:   }>;
948:   routeResult: ReturnType<typeof routeQuery>;
949:   budgetResult: ReturnType<typeof getDynamicTokenBudget>;
950:   s3meta: Sprint3PipelineMeta;
951:   fusedResults: HybridSearchResult[];
952:   vectorEmbeddingCache: Map<number, Float32Array>;
953: }
954: 
955: function resolveAdaptiveDocumentType(
956:   lists: Array<{ results: Array<Record<string, unknown>> }>,
957: ): string | undefined {
958:   const counts = new Map<string, number>();
959:   for (const list of lists) {
960:     for (const result of list.results.slice(0, 3)) {
961:       const candidate = result.documentType ?? result.document_type;
962:       if (typeof candidate !== 'string' || candidate.length === 0) {
963:         continue;
964:       }
965:       counts.set(candidate, (counts.get(candidate) ?? 0) + 1);
966:     }
967:   }
968: 
969:   let topType: string | undefined;
970:   let topCount = 0;
971:   for (const [documentType, count] of counts) {
972:     if (count > topCount) {
973:       topType = documentType;
974:       topCount = count;
975:     }
976:   }
977: 
978:   return topType;
979: }
980: 
981: function markFallbackRetry(results: HybridSearchResult[]): HybridSearchResult[] {
982:   for (const result of results) {
983:     (result as Record<string, unknown>).fallbackRetry = true;
984:   }
985: 
986:   return results;
987: }
988: 
989: function toEmbeddingBufferView(value: unknown): Float32Array | null {
990:   if (value instanceof Float32Array) {
991:     return value;
992:   }
993: 
994:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
995:     const asNumbers = value.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'number' && Number.isFinite(entry));
996:     return asNumbers ? new Float32Array(value) : null;
997:   }
998: 
999:   if (Buffer.isBuffer(value)) {
1000:     return new Float32Array(value.buffer, value.byteOffset, value.byteLength / 4);
1001:   }
1002: 
1003:   return null;
1004: }
1005: 
1006: async function executeFallbackPlan(
1007:   query: string,
1008:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1009:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {},
1010:   planKind: FallbackPlanKind,
1011:   overrides: Partial<HybridSearchOptions> = {}
1012: ): Promise<FallbackPlanExecution> {
1013:   const allowedChannels = getAllowedChannels(options);
1014:   const stages: FallbackPlanStage[] = [];
1015: 
1016:   const primaryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1017:     minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity ?? PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1018:     ...overrides,
1019:   });
1020:   const primaryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, primaryOptions);
1021:   const primaryResults = primaryExecution
1022:     ? applyResultLimit(primaryExecution.fusedResults, primaryOptions.limit)
1023:     : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, primaryOptions);
1024:   stages.push({
1025:     stage: 'primary',
1026:     options: primaryOptions,
1027:     results: primaryResults,
1028:     execution: primaryExecution,
1029:   });
1030: 
1031:   if (planKind === 'tiered') {
1032:     const trigger = checkDegradation(primaryResults);
1033:     if (!trigger) {
1034:       return { allowedChannels, stages };
1035:     }
1036: 
1037:     const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1038:       ...overrides,
1039:       minSimilarity: TIERED_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1040:       forceAllChannels: true,
1041:     });
1042:     const retryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1043:     const retryResults = retryExecution
1044:       ? applyResultLimit(retryExecution.fusedResults, retryOptions.limit)
1045:       : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1046:     stages.push({
1047:       stage: 'retry',
1048:       options: retryOptions,
1049:       results: retryResults,
1050:       execution: retryExecution,
1051:       trigger,
1052:     });
1053:     return { allowedChannels, stages };
1054:   }
1055: 
1056:   const primaryMinSimilarity = primaryOptions.minSimilarity ?? PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY;
1057:   if (primaryResults.length === 0 && primaryMinSimilarity >= SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY) {
1058:     const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1059:       ...overrides,
1060:       minSimilarity: SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1061:     });
1062:     const retryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1063:     const retryResultsBase = retryExecution
1064:       ? applyResultLimit(retryExecution.fusedResults, retryOptions.limit)
1065:       : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1066:     const retryResults = retryResultsBase.length > 0
1067:       ? markFallbackRetry(retryResultsBase)
1068:       : retryResultsBase;
1069:     stages.push({
1070:       stage: 'retry',
1071:       options: retryOptions,
1072:       results: retryResults,
1073:       execution: retryExecution,
1074:       trigger: 'empty',
1075:     });
1076:   }
1077: 
1078:   return { allowedChannels, stages };
1079: }
1080: 
1081: // 11. HYBRID SEARCH
1082: 
1083: /**
1084:  * Run multi-channel hybrid search combining vector, FTS, BM25, and graph results with per-source normalization.
1085:  * Prefer hybridSearchEnhanced() or searchWithFallback() instead. This function uses naive per-source
1086:  * min-max normalization which produces different orderings than the RRF pipeline in hybridSearchEnhanced().
1087:  * Retained as internal fallback only.
1088:  */
1089: async function hybridSearch(
1090:   query: string,
1091:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1092:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1093: ): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
1094:   const {
1095:     limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT,
1096:     specFolder,
1097:     minSimilarity = 0,
1098:     useBm25 = isBm25Enabled(),
1099:     useFts = true,
1100:     useVector = true,
1101:     useGraph = true,
1102:     includeArchived = false,
1103:   } = options;
1104: 
1105:   const results: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1106: 
1107:   // Vector search
1108:   if (useVector && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
1109:     try {
1110:       const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
1111:         limit,
1112:         specFolder,
1113:         minSimilarity,
1114:         includeConstitutional: false, // Handler manages constitutional separately
1115:         includeArchived,
1116:       });
1117:       for (const r of vectorResults) {
1118:         results.push({
1119:           ...r,
1120:           id: r.id as number,
1121:           score: (r.similarity as number) || 0,
1122:           source: 'vector',
1123:         });
1124:       }
1125:     } catch (error: unknown) {
1126:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1127:       console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Vector search failed: ${msg}`);
1128:     }
1129:   }
1130: 
1131:   // FTS search
1132:   if (useFts) {
1133:     const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
1134:     results.push(...ftsResults);
1135:   }
1136: 
1137:   // BM25 search
1138:   if (useBm25) {
1139:     const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, { limit, specFolder });
1140:     results.push(...bm25Results);
1141:   }
1142: 
1143:   // Graph search
1144:   if (useGraph && graphSearchFn) {
1145:     try {
1146:       const graphResults = graphSearchFn(query, { limit, specFolder });
1147:       for (const r of graphResults) {
1148:         results.push({
1149:           ...r,
1150:           id: r.id as number | string,
1151:           score: (r.score as number) || 0,
1152:           source: 'graph',
1153:         });
1154:       }
1155:     } catch (error: unknown) {
1156:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1157:       console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Graph search failed: ${msg}`);
1158:     }
1159:   }
1160: 
1161:   // Normalize scores per source before merging so one method's raw scale
1162:   // Does not dominate others during final ranking.
1163:   const bySource = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult[]>();
1164:   for (const r of results) {
1165:     const src = r.source || 'unknown';
1166:     if (!bySource.has(src)) bySource.set(src, []);
1167:     bySource.get(src)!.push(r); // non-null safe: has() guard above guarantees entry exists
1168:   }
1169: 
1170:   const normalized: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1171:   for (const [, group] of bySource) {
1172:     if (group.length === 0) continue;
1173:     const scores = group.map(r => r.score);
1174:     let min = Infinity;
1175:     let max = -Infinity;
1176:     for (const s of scores) {
1177:       if (s < min) min = s;
1178:       if (s > max) max = s;
1179:     }
1180:     const range = max - min;
1181:     for (const r of group) {
1182:       normalized.push({
1183:         ...r,
1184:         score: range > 0 ? (r.score - min) / range : (r.score > 0 ? 1.0 : 0),
1185:       });
1186:     }
1187:   }
1188: 
1189:   // Deduplicate by ID (keep highest normalized score)
1190:   // LIMITATION: When a result appears in multiple sources (e.g., vector + fts),
1191:   // Only the highest-scoring entry's `source` is preserved. Multi-source provenance
1192:   // Is lost here. To fix properly, HybridSearchResult would need a `sources: string[]`
1193:   // Field and downstream consumers would need to be updated accordingly.
1194:   const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
1195:   for (const r of normalized) {
1196:     const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
1197:     const existing = deduped.get(canonicalId);
1198:     if (!existing || r.score > existing.score) {
1199:       deduped.set(canonicalId, r);
1200:     }
1201:   }
1202: 
1203:   return Array.from(deduped.values())
1204:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
1205:     .slice(0, limit);
1206: }
1207: 
1208: /**
1209:  * Enhanced hybrid search with RRF fusion.
1210:  * All search channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
1211:  * is correct — Promise.all would add overhead without achieving parallelism.
1212:  */
1213: async function hybridSearchEnhanced(
1214:   query: string,
1215:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1216:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1217: ): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
1218:   const execution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, options);
1219:   if (execution) {
1220:     if (options.stopAfterFusion) {
1221:       return applyResultLimit(execution.fusedResults, options.limit);
1222:     }
1223: 
1224:     return enrichFusedResults(query, execution, options);
1225:   }
1226: 
1227:   return hybridSearch(query, embedding, options);
1228: }
1229: 
1230: async function collectAndFuseHybridResults(
1231:   query: string,
1232:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1233:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1234: ): Promise<HybridFusionExecution | null> {
1235:   try {
1236:     const evaluationMode = options.evaluationMode === true;
1237:     const lists: HybridFusionExecution['lists'] = [];
1238: 
1239:     // Pipeline metadata collector (populated by flag-gated stages)
1240:     const s3meta: Sprint3PipelineMeta = {};
1241: 
1242:     // -- Stage A: Query Classification + Routing (SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER) --
1243:     // When enabled, classifies query complexity and restricts channels to a
1244:     // Subset (e.g., simple queries skip graph+degree). When disabled, all channels run.
1245:     const routeResult = routeQuery(query, options.triggerPhrases);
1246:     const allPossibleChannels: ChannelName[] = ['vector', 'fts', 'bm25', 'graph', 'degree'];
1247:     const activeChannels = options.forceAllChannels
1248:       ? new Set<ChannelName>(allPossibleChannels)
1249:       : new Set<ChannelName>(routeResult.channels);
1250: 
1251:     // Respect explicit caller channel disables across both the primary route and
1252:     // every fallback tier. useGraph=false also disables the dependent degree lane.
1253:     const allowedChannels = getAllowedChannels(options);
1254:     for (const channel of allPossibleChannels) {
1255:       if (!allowedChannels.has(channel)) activeChannels.delete(channel);
1256:     }
1257: 
1258:     const skippedChannels = allPossibleChannels.filter(ch => !activeChannels.has(ch));
1259: 
1260:     if (skippedChannels.length > 0) {
1261:       s3meta.routing = {
1262:         tier: routeResult.tier,
1263:         channels: routeResult.channels,
1264:         skippedChannels,
1265:         featureFlagEnabled: isComplexityRouterEnabled(),
1266:         confidence: routeResult.classification.confidence,
1267:         features: routeResult.classification.features as Record<string, unknown>,
1268:       };
1269:     }
1270: 
1271:     // -- Stage E: Dynamic Token Budget (SPECKIT_DYNAMIC_TOKEN_BUDGET) --
1272:     // Compute tier-aware budget early so it's available for downstream truncation.
1273:     // When disabled, getDynamicTokenBudget returns the default 4000 budget with applied=false.
1274:     const budgetResult = getDynamicTokenBudget(routeResult.tier);
1275:     if (budgetResult.applied && !evaluationMode) {
1276:       s3meta.tokenBudget = {
1277:         tier: budgetResult.tier,
1278:         budget: budgetResult.budget,
1279:         applied: budgetResult.applied,
1280:         featureFlagEnabled: isDynamicTokenBudgetEnabled(),
1281:         configValues: DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET_CONFIG as unknown as Record<string, number>,
1282:         // headerOverhead and adjustedBudget are patched in below after they are computed
1283:         headerOverhead: 0,
1284:         adjustedBudget: budgetResult.budget,
1285:       };
1286:     }
1287: 
1288:     // Channel results collected independently, merged after all complete
1289:     let semanticResults: Array<{ id: number | string; source: string; [key: string]: unknown }> = [];
1290:     let ftsChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1291:     let bm25ChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1292:     let triggerChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1293:     const vectorEmbeddingCache = new Map<number, Float32Array>();
1294: 
1295:     // All channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
1296:     // Is correct — Promise.all adds overhead without parallelism.
1297: 
1298:     // Vector channel — gated by query-complexity routing
1299:     if (activeChannels.has('vector') && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
1300:       try {
1301:         const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
1302:           limit: options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT,
1303:           specFolder: options.specFolder,
1304:           minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity || 0,
1305:           includeConstitutional: false,
1306:           includeArchived: options.includeArchived || false,
1307:           includeEmbeddings: true,
1308:         });
1309:         semanticResults = vectorResults.map((r: Record<string, unknown>): { id: number | string; source: string; [key: string]: unknown } => ({
1310:           ...r,
1311:           id: r.id as number | string,
1312:           source: 'vector',
1313:         }));
1314:         for (const result of semanticResults) {
1315:           if (typeof result.id !== 'number') continue;
1316:           const embeddingCandidate = toEmbeddingBufferView(
1317:             (result as Record<string, unknown>).embedding
1318:             ?? (result as Record<string, unknown>).embeddingBuffer
1319:           );
1320:           if (embeddingCandidate) {
1321:             vectorEmbeddingCache.set(result.id, embeddingCandidate);
1322:           }
1323:         }
1324:         lists.push({ source: 'vector', results: semanticResults, weight: 1.0 });
1325:       } catch (_err: unknown) {
1326:         // Non-critical — vector channel failure does not block pipeline
1327:         console.warn('[hybrid-search] Channel error:', _err instanceof Error ? _err.message : String(_err));
1328:       }
1329:     }
1330: 
1331:     // FTS channel (internal error handling in ftsSearch) — gated by query-complexity routing
1332:     if (activeChannels.has('fts')) {
1333:       ftsChannelResults = ftsSearch(query, options);
1334:       if (ftsChannelResults.length > 0) {
1335:         // FTS weight reduced to 0.3 after ablation showed 0.8 was harmful,
1336:         // flooding top-K with noisy lexical matches despite its exact-match value.
1337:         lists.push({ source: 'fts', results: ftsChannelResults, weight: 0.3 });

1341:     // BM25 channel (internal error handling in bm25Search) — gated by query-complexity routing
1342:     if (activeChannels.has('bm25')) {

1344:       if (bm25ChannelResults.length > 0) {
1345:         // BM25 weight 0.6 is lowest lexical channel — in-memory BM25 index
1346:         // Has less precise scoring than SQLite FTS5 BM25; kept for coverage breadth.
1347:         lists.push({ source: 'bm25', results: bm25ChannelResults, weight: 0.6 });

1349:     }
1350:

1371:           })), weight: 0.5 });

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: DB Shard Migration
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import * as fs from 'fs';
6: import * as path from 'path';
7: 
8: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
9: import * as sqliteVec from 'sqlite-vec';
10: 
11: import type { EmbeddingProfile } from '@spec-kit/shared/embeddings/profile';
12: 
13: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: // 1. CONSTANTS
15: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: 
17: const SHARD_EXACT_TABLES = new Set(['vec_memories', 'embedding_cache']);
18: const CANONICAL_SKIP_PREFIXES = ['vec_memories'];
19: 
20: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: // 2. HELPERS
22: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
23: 
24: function quoteSqlString(value: string): string {
25:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, "''")}'`;
26: }
27: 
28: function quoteIdent(value: string): string {
29:   return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
30: }
31: 
32: function listUserTables(db: Database.Database, schema = 'main'): string[] {
33:   const tableRows = db.prepare(`
34:     SELECT name
35:     FROM ${quoteIdent(schema)}.sqlite_master
36:     WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
37:       AND name NOT LIKE 'sqlite_%'
38:     ORDER BY name
39:   `).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
40:   return tableRows.map((row) => row.name);
41: }
42: 
43: function isShardTable(tableName: string): boolean {
44:   return SHARD_EXACT_TABLES.has(tableName)
45:     || tableName.startsWith('vec_')
46:     || CANONICAL_SKIP_PREFIXES.some((prefix) => tableName.startsWith(`${prefix}_`));
47: }
48: 
49: function copyTable(db: Database.Database, fromSchema: string, toSchema: string, tableName: string): void {
50:   const source = `${quoteIdent(fromSchema)}.${quoteIdent(tableName)}`;
51:   const target = `${quoteIdent(toSchema)}.${quoteIdent(tableName)}`;
52:   db.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ${target}`);
53:   db.exec(`CREATE TABLE ${target} AS SELECT * FROM ${source}`);
54: }
55: 
56: function getTableColumns(db: Database.Database, schema: string, tableName: string): Set<string> {
57:   const columns = db.prepare(`PRAGMA ${quoteIdent(schema)}.table_info(${quoteIdent(tableName)})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
58:   return new Set(columns.map((column) => column.name));
59: }
60: 
61: function copyShardPayloadTable(
62:   db: Database.Database,
63:   tableName: string,
64:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
65: ): void {
66:   if (tableName.startsWith('vec_memories_')) {
67:     return;
68:   }
69:   if (tableName === 'vec_memories') {
70:     db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shard.vec_memories');
71:     db.exec(`
72:       CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE shard.vec_memories USING vec0(
73:         embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
74:       )
75:     `);
76:     const rows = db.prepare('SELECT rowid, embedding FROM legacy.vec_memories').all() as Array<{ rowid: number; embedding: Buffer }>;
77:     const insert = db.prepare('INSERT INTO shard.vec_memories(rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)');
78:     for (const row of rows) {
79:       insert.run(BigInt(row.rowid), row.embedding);
80:     }
81:     return;
82:   }
83:   if (tableName === 'embedding_cache') {
84:     const columns = getTableColumns(db, 'legacy', 'embedding_cache');
85:     const profileKeyExpr = columns.has('profile_key') ? "COALESCE(profile_key, '')" : "''";
86:     const inputKindExpr = columns.has('input_kind') ? "COALESCE(input_kind, 'document')" : "'document'";
87:     db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shard.embedding_cache');
88:     db.exec(`
89:       CREATE TABLE shard.embedding_cache (
90:         content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
91:         profile_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
92:         input_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'document' CHECK (input_kind IN ('document', 'query')),
93:         model_id TEXT NOT NULL,
94:         embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
95:         dimensions INTEGER NOT NULL,
96:         created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
97:         last_used_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
98:         PRIMARY KEY (content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, dimensions)
99:       )
100:     `);
101:     db.exec(`
102:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO shard.embedding_cache (
103:         content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, embedding, dimensions, created_at, last_used_at
104:       )
105:       SELECT
106:         content_hash,
107:         ${profileKeyExpr},
108:         ${inputKindExpr},
109:         model_id,
110:         embedding,
111:         dimensions,
112:         created_at,
113:         last_used_at
114:       FROM legacy.embedding_cache
115:     `);
116:     return;
117:   }
118:   copyTable(db, 'legacy', 'shard', tableName);
119: }
120: 
121: function dropCanonicalShardPayloadTables(db: Database.Database): void {
122:   const tables = listUserTables(db, 'main');
123:   for (const tableName of tables) {
124:     if (!isShardTable(tableName) || tableName === 'vec_metadata') {
125:       continue;
126:     }
127:     try {
128:       db.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS main.${quoteIdent(tableName)}`);
129:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
130:       // Best-effort canonical slimming; shard copy above is authoritative.
131:     }
132:   }
133: }
134: 
135: function moveWalCompanions(sourcePath: string, backupPath: string): void {
136:   for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
137:     const sourceCompanion = `${sourcePath}${suffix}`;
138:     if (!fs.existsSync(sourceCompanion)) {
139:       continue;
140:     }
141:     fs.renameSync(sourceCompanion, `${backupPath}${suffix}`);
142:   }
143: }
144: 
145: function legacyHasVectorTables(legacyPath: string): boolean {
146:   if (!fs.existsSync(legacyPath)) {
147:     return false;
148:   }
149: 
150:   const legacy = new Database(legacyPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
151:   try {
152:     const row = legacy.prepare(`
153:       SELECT 1 AS found
154:       FROM sqlite_master
155:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
156:         AND (name = 'vec_memories' OR name LIKE 'vec\\_%' ESCAPE '\\')
157:       LIMIT 1
158:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
159:     return row?.found === 1;
160:   } finally {
161:     legacy.close();
162:   }
163: }
164: 
165: function readShardMetadata(shardPath: string): Record<string, string> | null {
166:   if (!fs.existsSync(shardPath)) {
167:     return null;
168:   }
169: 
170:   const shard = new Database(shardPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
171:   try {
172:     const hasMetadata = shard.prepare(`
173:       SELECT 1 AS found
174:       FROM sqlite_master
175:       WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
176:       LIMIT 1
177:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
178:     if (hasMetadata?.found !== 1) {
179:       return null;
180:     }
181:     const rows = shard.prepare('SELECT key, value FROM vec_metadata').all() as Array<{ key: string; value: string }>;
182:     return Object.fromEntries(rows.map((row) => [row.key, row.value]));
183:   } finally {
184:     shard.close();
185:   }
186: }
187: 
188: function verifyExistingShard(profile: EmbeddingProfile, shardPath: string): void {
189:   const metadata = readShardMetadata(shardPath);
190:   if (!metadata) {
191:     return;
192:   }
193:   const mismatches = [
194:     ['provider', profile.provider],
195:     ['model', profile.model],
196:     ['dim', String(profile.dim)],
197:   ].filter(([key, expected]) => metadata[key] && metadata[key] !== expected);
198: 
199:   if (mismatches.length > 0) {
200:     const detail = mismatches.map(([key, expected]) => `${key}: expected ${expected}, got ${metadata[key]}`).join('; ');
201:     throw new Error(`Vector shard metadata mismatch for ${shardPath}: ${detail}`);
202:   }
203: }
204: 
205: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
206: // 3. CORE LOGIC
207: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
208: 
209: export function migrateLegacySingleDbToShardSync(
210:   legacyPath: string,
211:   canonicalPath: string,
212:   shardPath: string,
213:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
214: ): void {
215:   if (!legacyHasVectorTables(legacyPath)) {
216:     return;
217:   }
218: 
219:   if (fs.existsSync(canonicalPath) && fs.existsSync(shardPath)) {
220:     verifyExistingShard(profile, shardPath);
221:     console.error(`[db-shard-migration] canonical and shard already exist for ${profile.slug}; skipping migration`);
222:     return;
223:   }
224: 
225:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(canonicalPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
226:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(shardPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
227: 
228:   console.error(`[db-shard-migration] migrating legacy profile DB: ${legacyPath}`);
229:   if (!fs.existsSync(canonicalPath)) {
230:     fs.copyFileSync(legacyPath, canonicalPath);
231:     for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
232:       if (fs.existsSync(`${legacyPath}${suffix}`)) {
233:         fs.copyFileSync(`${legacyPath}${suffix}`, `${canonicalPath}${suffix}`);
234:       }
235:     }
236:   }
237: 
238:   const canonical = new Database(canonicalPath);
239:   try {
240:     canonical.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
241:     canonical.pragma('busy_timeout = 10000');
242:     sqliteVec.load(canonical);
243:     canonical.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ${quoteSqlString(legacyPath)} AS legacy`);
244:     canonical.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ${quoteSqlString(shardPath)} AS shard`);
245:     canonical.exec('PRAGMA shard.journal_mode = WAL');
246: 
247:     const legacyTables = listUserTables(canonical, 'legacy');
248:     for (const tableName of legacyTables) {
249:       if (tableName === 'vec_metadata') {
250:         continue;
251:       }
252:       if (isShardTable(tableName)) {
253:         copyShardPayloadTable(canonical, tableName, profile);
254:       }
255:     }
256: 
257:     canonical.exec(`
258:       DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shard.vec_metadata;
259:       CREATE TABLE shard.vec_metadata (
260:         key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
261:         value TEXT NOT NULL,
262:         created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
263:       );
264:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO shard.vec_metadata (key, value) VALUES
265:         ('provider', ${quoteSqlString(profile.provider)}),
266:         ('model', ${quoteSqlString(profile.model)}),
267:         ('dim', ${quoteSqlString(String(profile.dim))}),
268:         ('embedding_dim', ${quoteSqlString(String(profile.dim))});
269:     `);
270:     dropCanonicalShardPayloadTables(canonical);
271: 
272:     canonical.exec('DETACH DATABASE shard');
273:     canonical.exec('DETACH DATABASE legacy');
274:     canonical.exec('VACUUM');
275:   } finally {
276:     canonical.close();
277:   }
278: 
279:   const shard = new Database(shardPath);
280:   try {
281:     shard.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
282:     shard.exec('VACUUM');
283:   } finally {
284:     shard.close();
285:   }
286: 
287:   const migrationDir = path.join(path.dirname(canonicalPath), 'migrations');
288:   fs.mkdirSync(migrationDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
289:   const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[-:.TZ]/g, '').slice(0, 14);
290:   const backupPath = path.join(migrationDir, `legacy_${profile.slug}_${timestamp}.sqlite.bak`);
291:   fs.renameSync(legacyPath, backupPath);
292:   moveWalCompanions(legacyPath, backupPath);
293:   console.error(`[db-shard-migration] legacy DB moved to rollback backup: ${backupPath}`);
294: }
295: 
296: export async function migrateLegacySingleDbToShard(
297:   legacyPath: string,
298:   canonicalPath: string,
299:   shardPath: string,
300:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
301: ): Promise<void> {
302:   migrateLegacySingleDbToShardSync(legacyPath, canonicalPath, shardPath, profile);
303: }

(End of file - total 303 lines)
</content>

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Confidence Scoring
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Per-result calibrated confidence scoring
5: //
6: // PURPOSE: Combine margin, multi-channel agreement,
7: // and anchor density into a single calibrated confidence score per
8: // result. V1 uses heuristic scoring only — no LLM calls in the hot path.
9: //
10: // FEATURE FLAG: SPECKIT_RESULT_CONFIDENCE_V1 (default ON, graduated)
11: //
12: // OUTPUT SHAPE (per result):
13: // {
14: //   "confidence": {
15: //     "label": "high" | "medium" | "low",
16: //     "value": 0.78,
17: //     "drivers": ["large_margin", "multi_channel_agreement"]
18: //   },
19: //   "requestQuality": {
20: //     "label": "good" | "weak" | "gap"
21: //   }
22: // }
23: //
24: // IMPORTANT: This module only models ranking confidence for retrieval ordering.
25: // It is not freshness authority and it is not a substitute for StructuralTrust.
26: import { resolveEffectiveScore, type PipelineRow } from './pipeline/types.js';
27: 
28: declare const rankingConfidenceBrand: unique symbol;
29: 
30: // -- Constants --
31: 
32: const HIGH_THRESHOLD = 0.7;
33: const LOW_THRESHOLD = 0.4;
34: 
35: // Weights for each active confidence factor. The former reranker weight (0.20)
36: // was removed with the LLM reranker; its term was already inert (always 0), so
37: // rawValue stays capped at 0.80 exactly as before — behavior-neutral. The 0.20
38: // is intentionally NOT redistributed (that would change confidence scores).
39: const WEIGHT_MARGIN = 0.35;
40: const WEIGHT_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT = 0.30;
41: const WEIGHT_ANCHOR_DENSITY = 0.15;
42: 
43: // Margin thresholds (gap between top score and next score, 0–1 scale)
44: const LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15;
45: const SMALL_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.05;
46: 
47: // Channel count thresholds
48: const STRONG_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT_MIN = 2;
49: 
50: // -- Types --
51: 
52: /** Confidence label for a single result. */
53: export type ConfidenceLabel = 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Branded ranking score for retrieval ordering only.
57:  * Consumers must not reuse this as parser provenance, evidence status,
58:  * freshness authority, or any other StructuralTrust axis.
59:  */
60: export type RankingConfidenceValue = number & {
61:   readonly [rankingConfidenceBrand]: 'RankingConfidenceValue';
62: };
63: 
64: /** Quality label at the request level (across all results). */
65: export type RequestQualityLabel = 'good' | 'weak' | 'gap';
66: 
67: /** Which factors drove the confidence score upward. */
68: export type ConfidenceDriver =
69:   | 'large_margin'
70:   | 'multi_channel_agreement'
71:   | 'anchor_density';
72: 
73: /** Per-result confidence payload. */
74: export interface RankingConfidenceContract {
75:   label: ConfidenceLabel;
76:   value: RankingConfidenceValue;
77:   drivers: ConfidenceDriver[];
78:   structuralTrust?: never;
79:   parserProvenance?: never;
80:   evidenceStatus?: never;
81:   freshnessAuthority?: never;
82: }
83: 
84: export interface ResultConfidence {
85:   confidence: RankingConfidenceContract;
86: }
87: 
88: /** Request-level quality assessment (one per search call). */
89: export interface RequestQualityAssessment {
90:   requestQuality: {
91:     label: RequestQualityLabel;
92:   };
93: }
94: 
95: /**
96:  * Minimal result shape needed for confidence computation.
97:  * Uses `Record<string, unknown>` to stay compatible with both
98:  * `RawSearchResult` and `PipelineRow` without importing either.
99:  */
100: export interface ScoredResult extends Record<string, unknown> {
101:   id: number;
102:   /** Composite score (0–1). */
103:   score?: number;
104:   /** RRF fusion score (0–1). */
105:   rrfScore?: number;
106:   /** Intent-adjusted score (0–1). */
107:   intentAdjustedScore?: number;
108:   /** Raw cosine similarity (0–100 scale from sqlite-vec). */
109:   similarity?: number;
110:   /** Score origin metadata for distinguishing real reranks from fallbacks. */
111:   scoringMethod?: string;
112:   /** Anchor metadata array populated by Stage 2. */
113:   anchorMetadata?: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
114:   /** Source channels that contributed this result. */
115:   sources?: string[];
116:   /** Single source channel string (legacy). */
117:   source?: string;
118:   /** Trace metadata containing channel attribution. */
119:   traceMetadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
120: }
121: 
122: // -- Internal helpers --
123: 
124: function resolveScore(result: ScoredResult): number {
125:   return resolveEffectiveScore(result as PipelineRow);
126: }
127: 
128: /**
129:  * Count the number of unique channels that retrieved this result.
130:  * Inspects `sources`, `source`, and traceMetadata.attribution.
131:  */
132: function countChannels(result: ScoredResult): number {
133:   const channels = new Set<string>();
134: 
135:   if (Array.isArray(result.sources)) {
136:     for (const s of result.sources) {
137:       if (typeof s === 'string' && s.trim().length > 0) channels.add(s.trim());
138:     }
139:   }
140: 
141:   if (typeof result.source === 'string' && result.source.trim().length > 0) {
142:     channels.add(result.source.trim());
143:   }
144: 
145:   // Check traceMetadata.attribution for richer channel data
146:   const numericId = typeof result.id === 'number' ? result.id : null;
147:   const attribution = result.traceMetadata?.attribution;
148:   if (attribution && typeof attribution === 'object' && numericId !== null) {
149:     for (const [channel, memoryIds] of Object.entries(attribution as Record<string, unknown>)) {
150:       if (!Array.isArray(memoryIds)) continue;
151:       const matched = memoryIds.some((mid) => {
152:         const n = typeof mid === 'number' ? mid : typeof mid === 'string' ? Number(mid) : NaN;
153:         return Number.isFinite(n) && n === numericId;
154:       });
155:       if (matched && typeof channel === 'string' && channel.trim().length > 0) {
156:         channels.add(channel.trim());
157:       }
158:     }
159:   }
160: 
161:   return channels.size;
162: }
163: 
164: /**
165:  * Count anchors present in this result's anchorMetadata array.
166:  * A result with multiple named anchors is considered "dense" and
167:  * more likely to be a high-quality, well-structured memory.
168:  */
169: function countAnchors(result: ScoredResult): number {
170:   if (!Array.isArray(result.anchorMetadata)) return 0;
171:   return result.anchorMetadata.length;
172: }
173: 
174: /**
175:  * Compute the score margin between result[i] and result[i+1].
176:  * Tail results have no successor, so they receive no synthetic margin boost.
177:  */
178: function computeMargin(score: number, nextScore: number | null): number {
179:   if (nextScore === null) return 0;
180:   const gap = score - nextScore;
181:   return Math.max(0, gap);
182: }
183: 
184: /**
185:  * Map raw confidence value to a coarse label.
186:  */
187: function toConfidenceLabel(value: number): ConfidenceLabel {
188:   if (value >= HIGH_THRESHOLD) return 'high';
189:   if (value >= LOW_THRESHOLD) return 'medium';
190:   return 'low';
191: }
192: 
193: export function asRankingConfidenceValue(value: number): RankingConfidenceValue {
194:   const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
195:   return (Math.round(clamped * 1000) / 1000) as RankingConfidenceValue;
196: }
197: 
198: // -- Public API --
199: 
200: /**
201:  * Compute per-result confidence for a ranked list of results.
202:  *
203:  * Each result receives a confidence object derived from:
204:  *   - Score margin to the next result (35% weight)
205:  *   - Number of channels that contributed this result (30%)
206:  *   - Anchor density in anchorMetadata (15%)
207:  *
208:  * @param results - Ranked results (highest score first). Ordering is assumed.
209:  * @returns Array of ResultConfidence objects, parallel to `results`.
210:  */
211: export function computeResultConfidence(results: ScoredResult[]): ResultConfidence[] {
212:   if (!Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) return [];
213: 
214:   const scores = results.map(resolveScore);
215: 
216:   return results.map((result, i): ResultConfidence => {
217:     const score = scores[i] ?? 0;
218:     const nextScore = i + 1 < scores.length ? (scores[i + 1] ?? null) : null;
219: 
220:     const margin = computeMargin(score, nextScore);
221:     const channelCount = countChannels(result);
222:     const anchorCount = countAnchors(result);
223: 
224:     // Factor scores (each 0–1)
225:     const marginFactor = margin >= LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD
226:       ? 1.0
227:       : margin >= SMALL_MARGIN_THRESHOLD
228:         ? margin / LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD
229:         : 0;
230: 
231:     const channelFactor = channelCount >= STRONG_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT_MIN
232:       ? 1.0
233:       : channelCount === 1
234:         ? 0.5
235:         : 0;
236: 
237:     // Anchor density: 1 anchor → 0.3, 2 → 0.6, 3+ → 1.0
238:     const anchorFactor = Math.min(1, anchorCount * 0.33);
239: 
240:     const rawValue =
241:       WEIGHT_MARGIN * marginFactor +
242:       WEIGHT_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT * channelFactor +
243:       WEIGHT_ANCHOR_DENSITY * anchorFactor;
244: 
245:     // Base score is a strong prior: if the score itself is very high, confidence
246:     // should reflect that even when heuristic signals are weak.
247:     const scorePrior = score * 0.4;
248:     const heuristicValue = rawValue * 0.6;
249:     const value = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, heuristicValue + scorePrior));
250: 
251:     const drivers: ConfidenceDriver[] = [];
252:     if (margin >= LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD) drivers.push('large_margin');
253:     if (channelCount >= STRONG_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT_MIN) drivers.push('multi_channel_agreement');
254:     if (anchorCount >= 2) drivers.push('anchor_density');
255: 
256:       return {
257:         confidence: {
258:           label: toConfidenceLabel(value),
259:           value: asRankingConfidenceValue(value),
260:           drivers,
261:         },
262:       };
263:   });
264: }
265: 
266: /**
267:  * Compute request-level quality assessment based on the overall result set.
268:  *
269:  * - "good":  most results have high/medium confidence and a healthy top score
270:  * - "weak":  results exist but signals are mixed or low
271:  * - "gap":   no results, or all results have low confidence
272:  *
273:  * @param results   - The scored results for the query.
274:  * @param confidences - Parallel confidence array from `computeResultConfidence`.
275:  */
276: export function assessRequestQuality(
277:   results: ScoredResult[],
278:   confidences: ResultConfidence[],
279: ): RequestQualityAssessment {
280:   if (!Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) {
281:     return { requestQuality: { label: 'gap' } };
282:   }
283: 
284:   const highOrMediumCount = confidences.filter(
285:     (c) => c.confidence.label === 'high' || c.confidence.label === 'medium',
286:   ).length;
287: 
288:   const topScore = resolveScore(results[0]);
289: 
290:   const qualityRatio = highOrMediumCount / results.length;
291: 
292:   if (topScore >= HIGH_THRESHOLD && qualityRatio >= 0.6) {
293:     return { requestQuality: { label: 'good' } };
294:   }
295:   if (results.length > 0 && (topScore >= LOW_THRESHOLD || qualityRatio >= 0.3)) {
296:     return { requestQuality: { label: 'weak' } };
297:   }
298:   return { requestQuality: { label: 'gap' } };
299: }
300: 
301: /**
302:  * Check whether the per-result confidence feature flag is enabled.
303:  * Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_RESULT_CONFIDENCE_V1=false to disable.
304:  */
305: export { isResultConfidenceEnabled } from './search-flags.js';

(End of file - total 305 lines)

29: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30: // 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS
31: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32: 
33: export type ReindexJobStatus = 'queued' | 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
34: 
35: export interface ReindexJob {
36:   readonly id: string;
37:   readonly fromName: string;
38:   readonly toName: string;
39:   readonly toDim: number;
40:   readonly total: number;
41:   readonly processed: number;

48: export interface StartReindexOptions {

50: }
51: 
52: export interface ReindexRuntimeOptions {
53:   readonly db?: Database.Database;
54: }
55: 
56: interface ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions extends ReindexRuntimeOptions {
57:   readonly autoStart?: boolean;
58: }
59: 
60: type JobRow = Omit<ReindexJob, 'error'> & {
61:   readonly error: string | null;
62: };

64: interface MemoryRow {
