71: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: // 2. CONSTANTS
73: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: 
75: const DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE = 50;
76: const MIN_BATCH_SIZE = 1;
77: const MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 1_000;
78: const RESTORE_REQUEUE_DELAY_MS = 100;
79: const logger = createLogger('embedder-reindex');
80: const JOB_TABLE_SQL = `
81:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS embedder_jobs (
82:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
83:     from_name TEXT NOT NULL,
84:     to_name TEXT NOT NULL,
85:     to_dim INTEGER NOT NULL,
86:     total INTEGER NOT NULL,
87:     processed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
88:     status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('queued', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled')),
89:     started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
90:     updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
91:     error TEXT
92:   )
93: `;
94: 
95: const JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS = `
96:   id,
97:   from_name AS fromName,
98:   to_name AS toName,
99:   to_dim AS toDim,
100:   total,
101:   processed,
102:   status,
103:   started_at AS startedAt,
104:   updated_at AS updatedAt,
105:   error
106: `;
107: 
108: const runningJobs = new Set<string>();
109: 
110: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
111: // 3. HELPERS
112: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
113: 
114: export class InvalidDatabaseDirError extends Error {
115:   constructor(message: string) {
116:     super(message);
117:     this.name = 'InvalidDatabaseDirError';
118:   }
119: }
120: 
121: function resolveDb(db?: Database.Database): Database.Database {
122:   return db ?? initializeDb();
123: }
124: 
125: function nowIso(): string {
126:   return new Date().toISOString();
127: }
128: 
129: function readJobStatus(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): ReindexJobStatus | null {
130:   ensureJobTable(db);
131:   const row = db.prepare('SELECT status FROM embedder_jobs WHERE id = ?').get(jobId) as
132:     | { status?: ReindexJobStatus }
133:     | undefined;
134:   return row?.status ?? null;
135: }
136: 
137: function getBatchSize(): number {
138:   return parseBoundedEnv('EMBEDDER_REINDEX_BATCH_SIZE', DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE, MIN_BATCH_SIZE, MAX_BATCH_SIZE);
139: }
140: 
141: function jobFromRow(row: JobRow): ReindexJob {
142:   const { error, ...job } = row;
143:   return error ? { ...job, error } : job;
144: }
145: 
146: function ensureJobTable(db: Database.Database): void {
147:   db.exec(JOB_TABLE_SQL);
148: }
149: 
150: function selectJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): ReindexJob | null {
151:   ensureJobTable(db);
152:   const row = db.prepare(`
153:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
154:     FROM embedder_jobs
155:     WHERE id = ?
156:   `).get(jobId) as JobRow | undefined;
157: 
158:   return row ? jobFromRow(row) : null;
159: }
160: 
161: function selectActiveJob(db: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
162:   ensureJobTable(db);
163:   const row = db.prepare(`
164:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
165:     FROM embedder_jobs
166:     WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
167:     ORDER BY started_at DESC, id DESC
168:     LIMIT 1
169:   `).get() as JobRow | undefined;
170: 
171:   return row ? jobFromRow(row) : null;
172: }
173: 
174: function setJobStatus(
175:   db: Database.Database,
176:   jobId: string,
177:   status: ReindexJobStatus,
178:   processed?: number,
179:   error?: string,
180: ): void {
181:   const params = {
182:     jobId,
183:     status,
184:     updatedAt: nowIso(),
185:     processed,
186:     error,
187:   };
188: 
189:   if (typeof processed === 'number' && typeof error === 'string') {
190:     db.prepare(`
191:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
192:       SET status = @status,
193:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
194:           processed = @processed,
195:           error = @error
196:       WHERE id = @jobId
197:     `).run(params);
198:     return;
199:   }
200: 
201:   if (typeof processed === 'number') {
202:     // A per-batch progress write must NOT clobber a terminal status set
203:     // by a concurrent cancelJob(). The only caller that passes processed without an error
204:     // is the in-loop 'running' progress update, which must only apply while the job is
205:     // still 'running' (or being re-affirmed as 'running'); never resurrect a 'cancelled'
206:     // (or 'completed'/'failed') row back to 'running'. Terminal transitions (completed /
207:     // failed) always carry the other branches and stay unconditional.
208:     if (status === 'running') {
209:       db.prepare(`
210:         UPDATE embedder_jobs
211:         SET status = @status,
212:             updated_at = @updatedAt,
213:             processed = @processed
214:         WHERE id = @jobId
215:           AND status = 'running'
216:       `).run(params);
217:       return;
218:     }
219:     db.prepare(`
220:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
221:       SET status = @status,
222:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
223:           processed = @processed
224:       WHERE id = @jobId
225:     `).run(params);
226:     return;
227:   }
228: 
229:   if (typeof error === 'string') {
230:     db.prepare(`
231:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
232:       SET status = @status,
233:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
234:           error = @error
235:       WHERE id = @jobId
236:     `).run(params);
237:     return;
238:   }
239: 
240:   db.prepare(`
241:     UPDATE embedder_jobs
242:     SET status = @status,
243:         updated_at = @updatedAt
244:     WHERE id = @jobId
245:   `).run(params);
246: }
247: 
248: function selectMemoryBatch(db: Database.Database, offset: number, limit: number): MemoryRow[] {
249:   return db.prepare(`
250:     SELECT id, content_text, title, file_path
251:     FROM memory_index
252:     ORDER BY id ASC
253:     LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
254:   `).all(limit, offset) as MemoryRow[];
255: }
256: 
257: function memoryText(row: MemoryRow): string {
258:   if (typeof row.content_text === 'string' && row.content_text.length > 0) {
259:     return row.content_text;
260:   }
261:   if (typeof row.title === 'string' && row.title.length > 0) {
262:     return row.title;
263:   }
264:   return row.file_path ?? '';
265: }
266: 
267: function getBatchPair(
268:   rows: MemoryRow[],
269:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
270:   index: number,
271: ): { readonly row: MemoryRow; readonly embedding: Float32Array } {
272:   const row = rows[index];
273:   const embedding = embeddings[index];
274:   if (!row || !embedding) {
275:     const missing: string[] = [];
276:     if (!row) missing.push('row=undefined');
277:     if (!embedding) missing.push('embedding=undefined');
278:     throw new Error(
279:       `Embedding batch entry missing at index ${index}: ${missing.join(', ')} ` +
280:         `(rows.length=${rows.length}, embeddings.length=${embeddings.length})`,
281:     );
282:   }
283:   return { row, embedding };
284: }
285: 
286: function writeVectors(
287:   db: Database.Database,
288:   tableName: string,
289:   rows: MemoryRow[],
290:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
291: ): void {
292:   const writeBatch = db.transaction(() => {
293:     const stmt = db.prepare(`
294:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${tableName} (id, vec)
295:       VALUES (?, ?)
296:     `);
297: 
298:     for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 1) {
299:       const { row, embedding } = getBatchPair(rows, embeddings, i);
300:       stmt.run(row.id, to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
301:     }
302:   });
303: 
304:   writeBatch();
305: }
306: 
307: function getDatabaseDir(db: Database.Database): string | null {
308:   const row = (db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>)
309:     .find((entry) => entry.name === 'main');
310:   if (!row?.file || row.file === ':memory:') {
311:     return null;
312:   }
313:   return path.dirname(row.file);
314: }
315: 
316: function getDatabasePath(db: Database.Database): string | null {
317:   try {
318:     const row = (db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>)
319:       .find((entry) => entry.name === 'main');
320:     if (!row?.file || row.file === ':memory:') {
321:       return null;
322:     }
323:     return row.file;
324:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
325:     return null;
326:   }
327: }
328: 
329: function isClosedDatabaseHandleError(error: unknown): boolean {
330:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
331:   return /database connection is not open|closed database|database handle is closed/i.test(message);
332: }
333: 
334: function scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId: string, dbPath: string | null): void {
335:   setTimeout(() => {
336:     // Do not re-open the database while a checkpoint restore is still in
337:     // progress: initialize_db runs interrupted-restore recovery, which would
338:     // roll back the in-flight restore. Wait for the barrier to clear.
339:     if (getRestoreBarrierStatus()) {
340:       scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
341:       return;
342:     }
343:     try {
344:       enqueueJob(dbPath ? initializeDb(dbPath) : initializeDb(), jobId);
345:     } catch (error: unknown) {
346:       logger.warn('embedder reindex restore retry skipped', {
347:         event: 'embedder_reindex_restore_retry_skipped',
348:         jobId,
349:         error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
350:       });
351:     }
352:   }, RESTORE_REQUEUE_DELAY_MS).unref?.();
353: }
354: 
355: function requireDatabaseDir(db: Database.Database, operation: string): string {
356:   const databaseDir = getDatabaseDir(db);
357:   if (!databaseDir) {
358:     throw new InvalidDatabaseDirError(
359:       `Embedder reindex ${operation} requires a file-backed database; received an in-memory or anonymous database without a database directory`,
360:     );
361:   }
362:   return databaseDir;
363: }
364: 
365: function writeVectorsToKnn(
366:   db: Database.Database,
367:   rows: MemoryRow[],
368:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
369: ): void {
370:   const writeBatch = db.transaction(() => {
371:     const del = db.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?');
372:     const ins = db.prepare('INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)');
373:     for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 1) {
374:       const { row, embedding } = getBatchPair(rows, embeddings, i);
375:       del.run(BigInt(row.id));
376:       ins.run(BigInt(row.id), to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
377:     }
378:   });
379: 
380:   writeBatch();
381: }
382: 
383: function ensureShardSchema(shard: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile, tableName: string): void {
384:   shard.exec(`
385:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_metadata (
386:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
387:       value TEXT NOT NULL,
388:       created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
389:     );
390:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${tableName} (
391:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
392:       vec BLOB NOT NULL
393:     );
394:   `);
395: 
396:   const writeMetadata = shard.transaction(() => {
397:     const stmt = shard.prepare('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO vec_metadata (key, value) VALUES (?, ?)');
398:     stmt.run('provider', profile.provider);
399:     stmt.run('model', profile.model);
400:     stmt.run('dim', String(profile.dim));
401:     stmt.run('embedding_dim', String(profile.dim));
402:   });
403:   writeMetadata();
404: }
405: 
406: function writeVectorsToShard(
407:   db: Database.Database,
408:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
409:   tableName: string,
410:   rows: MemoryRow[],
411:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
412:   shardPath?: string,
413: ): void {
414:   const databaseDir = requireDatabaseDir(db, 'vector shard write');
415: 
416:   // Writes target a caller-supplied STAGING shard path (default: the live
417:   // active shard for backward compatibility). runJob writes every batch to the staging
418:   // path and atomically renames staging -> active on success, so a mid-loop failure can
419:   // never leave the live (active) shard half-written.
420:   const shard = new Database(shardPath ?? profile.getVectorShardPath(databaseDir));
421:   try {
422:     shard.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
423: 
424:     // Load sqlite-vec so the runtime KNN virtual table can be created
425:     // and populated alongside the canonical vec_<dim> blob table. When the extension
426:     // is unavailable the runtime falls back to vec_<dim> only and search degrades to
427:     // lexical signals; we surface that through the existing console.warn in vector-index-store.
428:     let vecAvailable = true;
429:     try {
430:       sqliteVec.load(shard);
431:     } catch (_err) {
432:       vecAvailable = false;
433:     }
434: 
435:     ensureShardSchema(shard, profile, tableName);
436: 
437:     if (vecAvailable) {
438:       shard.exec(`
439:         CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_memories USING vec0(
440:           embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
441:         )
442:       `);
443:     }
444: 
445:     writeVectors(shard, tableName, rows, embeddings);
446:     if (vecAvailable) {
447:       writeVectorsToKnn(shard, rows, embeddings);
448:     }
449:   } finally {
450:     // WAL-durability: flush + TRUNCATE the shard WAL before close so the shard file is
451:     // consistent at rest with an empty WAL — mirrors close_db's corruption-prevention.
452:     // better-sqlite3 .close() only does a passive checkpoint; an abrupt later kill could otherwise
453:     // corrupt un-checkpointed shard frames (vec0 / FTS segment writes). Best-effort; never block close.
454:     try { shard.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch (_err: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
455:     shard.close();
456:   }
457: }
458: 
459: function enqueueJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): void {
460:   if (runningJobs.has(jobId)) {
461:     return;
462:   }
463: 
464:   runningJobs.add(jobId);
465:   void runJob(db, jobId).finally(() => {
466:     runningJobs.delete(jobId);
467:   });
468: }
469: 
470: async function runJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): Promise<void> {
471:   const dbPath = getDatabasePath(db);
472:   let jobDb = db;
473:   if (getRestoreBarrierStatus()) {
474:     scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
475:     return;
476:   }
477:   const resolveJobDb = (): Database.Database => {
478:     const barrierStatus = getRestoreBarrierStatus();
479:     if (barrierStatus) {
480:       const error = new Error(barrierStatus.message);
481:       error.name = barrierStatus.code;
482:       throw error;
483:     }
484:     return jobDb;
485:   };
486:   jobDb = resolveJobDb();
487:   const initialJob = selectJob(jobDb, jobId);
488:   if (!initialJob || initialJob.status === 'completed' || initialJob.status === 'failed' || initialJob.status === 'cancelled') {
489:     return;
490:   }
491: 
492:   const manifest = getManifest(initialJob.toName);
493:   if (!manifest) {
494:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'failed', undefined, `UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER: ${initialJob.toName}`);
495:     return;
496:   }
497:   const adapter = getEmbedderAdapter(manifest.backend, initialJob.toName, initialJob.toDim);
498:   const targetProfile = new EmbeddingProfile({
499:     provider: manifest.backend,
500:     model: manifest.name,
501:     dim: manifest.dim,
502:     dtype: null,
503:     baseUrl: null,
504:   });
505: 
506:   ensureVecTableForDim(jobDb, initialJob.toDim);
507:   const tableName = vecTableNameForDim(initialJob.toDim);
508:   const batchSize = getBatchSize();
509:   let processed = initialJob.processed;
510: 
511:   // Stage every shard write into a per-job staging file, then atomically
512:   // rename it over the live active shard ONLY after the completion transaction commits.
513:   // A mid-loop throw (or cancel) leaves the live (active) shard untouched; the partial
514:   // staging artifact is unlinked in the catch/cancel paths. databaseDir is guaranteed
515:   // file-backed here (startReindexInternal already called requireDatabaseDir).
516:   const databaseDir = requireDatabaseDir(jobDb, 'reindex staging');
517:   const activeShardPath = targetProfile.getVectorShardPath(databaseDir);
518:   const stagingShardPath = `${activeShardPath}.reindex-${jobId}.staging`;
519: 
520:   const cleanupStaging = (): void => {
521:     for (const suffix of ['', '-wal', '-shm']) {
522:       try {
523:         fs.unlinkSync(`${stagingShardPath}${suffix}`);
524:       } catch (_err: unknown) {
525:         /* best-effort: staging may not exist */
526:       }
527:     }
528:   };
529: 
530:   try {
531:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'running');
532:     // Start each run from a clean staging slate (a prior crashed run may have left one).
533:     cleanupStaging();
534: 
535:     while (processed < initialJob.total) {
536:       jobDb = resolveJobDb();
537:       // Re-read the live cancel/status before each batch so cancelJob()
538:       // during a run stops the worker before the next write. Abort cleanly: drop the
539:       // staging artifact and leave the live shard + active pointer untouched.
540:       if (readJobStatus(jobDb, jobId) === 'cancelled') {
541:         cleanupStaging();
542:         return;
543:       }
544: 
545:       const rows = selectMemoryBatch(jobDb, processed, batchSize);
546:       if (rows.length === 0) {
547:         break;
548:       }
549: 
550:       const embeddings = await adapter.embed(rows.map(memoryText));
551:       jobDb = resolveJobDb();
552:       if (embeddings.length !== rows.length) {
553:         throw new Error(`Embedder returned ${embeddings.length} embeddings for ${rows.length} memories`);
554:       }
555: 
556:       writeVectors(jobDb, tableName, rows, embeddings);
557:       writeVectorsToShard(jobDb, targetProfile, tableName, rows, embeddings, stagingShardPath);
558:       processed += rows.length;
559:       // Clobber-safe: only advances while status is still 'running' (never resurrects
560:       // a concurrently-set 'cancelled').
561:       setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'running', processed);
562:       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
563:         setImmediate(resolve);
564:       });
565:     }
566: 
567:     // Final cancel checkpoint before the irreversible swap/commit.
568:     jobDb = resolveJobDb();
569:     if (readJobStatus(jobDb, jobId) === 'cancelled') {
570:       cleanupStaging();
571:       return;
572:     }
573: 
574:     // Atomically swap the staged shard over the live active shard BEFORE the
575:     // completion transaction flips the active-embedder pointer. rename(2) is atomic on
576:     // the same filesystem; the staging file always lives beside the active shard, so the
577:     // active shard either is the old file (failure) or the fully-staged new file (success),
578:     // never a half-written mix. If no batch produced a staging file (e.g. zero rows), skip.
579:     if (fs.existsSync(stagingShardPath)) {
580:       // Drop any stale sidecars next to the active shard so the renamed WAL/SHM (already
581:       // TRUNCATE-checkpointed at staging close) is the authoritative pair.
582:       for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
583:         try {
584:           fs.unlinkSync(`${activeShardPath}${suffix}`);
585:         } catch (_err: unknown) {
586:           /* best-effort */
587:         }
588:       }
589:       fs.renameSync(stagingShardPath, activeShardPath);
590:     }
591: 
592:     const complete = jobDb.transaction(() => {
593:       // Persist the active-embedder provider pointer too; omitting it leaves the pointer empty,
594:       // which readActiveEmbedderIfValid coerces to undefined and the next boot loses provider
595:       // identity. 'api' backends are ambiguous (openai vs voyage) from the manifest alone, so
596:       // leave the provider unset for those rather than guess.
597:       const activeProvider = manifest.backend === 'ollama'
598:         ? 'ollama'
599:         : manifest.backend === 'sentence-transformers'
600:           ? 'hf-local'
601:           : undefined;
602:       setActiveEmbedder(jobDb, initialJob.toName, initialJob.toDim, activeProvider);
603:       // Commit embedding_status for rows now backed by an active-profile vector.
604:       // A vector-only reindex previously wrote vectors but left memory_index.embedding_status
605:       // stale, so a "completed" bulk re-embed never raised the success count. Reconcile the
606:       // status for every row present in the just-written vec_<dim> table, inside the same
607:       // atomic completion transaction so vectors and status flip together.
608:       const completedAt = nowIso();
609:       jobDb.prepare(`
610:         UPDATE memory_index
611:         SET embedding_status = 'success',
612:             embedding_generated_at = COALESCE(embedding_generated_at, @ts),
613:             updated_at = @ts,
614:             failure_reason = NULL
615:         WHERE embedding_status != 'success'
616:           AND id IN (SELECT id FROM ${tableName})
617:       `).run({ ts: completedAt });
618:       setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'completed', initialJob.total);
619:     });
620:     complete();
621:     // The active-embedder pointer just flipped; drop the cached provider singleton so the
622:     // next embedding re-resolves against the new pointer instead of the stale model/dim.
623:     invalidateProviderSingleton();
624:     void teardownEmbedderAfterSwap(manifest.backend, initialJob.toName).catch((error: unknown) => {
625:       console.warn(`[embedder-reindex] embedder teardown after swap failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
626:     });
627:     if (getDatabaseDir(jobDb)) {
628:       attachActiveVectorShard(jobDb, targetProfile);
629:     }
630:   } catch (error: unknown) {
631:     if (getRestoreBarrierStatus() || isClosedDatabaseHandleError(error)) {
632:       cleanupStaging();
633:       scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
634:       logger.warn('embedder reindex paused for checkpoint restore', {
635:         event: 'embedder_reindex_restore_paused',
636:         jobId,
637:         processed,
638:       });
639:       return;
640:     }
641:     // A failed run must not leave a half-written shard anywhere. The live active
642:     // shard was never mutated (all writes went to staging); discard the partial staging
643:     // artifact so the next attempt starts clean and the active shard stays the last-good one.
644:     cleanupStaging();
645:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
646:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'failed', processed, message);
647:     logger.error('embedder reindex job failed', {
648:       event: 'embedder_reindex_failed',
649:       jobId,
650:       toName: initialJob.toName,
651:       processed,
652:       total: initialJob.total,
653:       error: message,
654:     });
655:   }
656: }
657: 
658: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
659: // 4. CORE LOGIC
660: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
661: 
662: export function startReindex(
663:   options: StartReindexOptions,
664:   runtimeOptions: ReindexRuntimeOptions = {},
665: ): string {
666:   return startReindexInternal(options, runtimeOptions);
667: }
668: 
669: function startReindexInternal(
670:   options: StartReindexOptions,
671:   runtimeOptions: ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions = {},
672: ): string {
673:   const db = resolveDb(runtimeOptions.db);
674:   requireDatabaseDir(db, 'startup');
675:   ensureJobTable(db);
676: 
677:   const manifest = getManifest(options.toName);
678:   if (!manifest) {
679:     const error = new Error(`UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER: ${options.toName}`);
680:     error.name = 'UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER';
681:     throw error;
682:   }
683: 
684:   ensureVecTableForDim(db, manifest.dim);
685:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(db);
686:   const totalRow = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index').get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
687:   const total = Number(totalRow?.count ?? 0);
688:   const timestamp = nowIso();
689:   const id = `emb-swap-${timestamp.replace(/[:.]/g, '-')}-${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;
690: 
691:   db.prepare(`
692:     INSERT INTO embedder_jobs (
693:       id, from_name, to_name, to_dim, total, processed, status, started_at, updated_at, error
694:     ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0, 'queued', ?, ?, NULL)
695:   `).run(id, active.name, manifest.name, manifest.dim, total, timestamp, timestamp);
696: 
697:   if (runtimeOptions.autoStart !== false) {
698:     enqueueJob(db, id);
699:   }
700: 
701:   return id;
702: }
703: 
704: export function getJobStatus(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
705:   return selectJob(resolveDb(db), jobId);
706: }
707: 
708: export function getActiveJob(db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
709:   return selectActiveJob(resolveDb(db));
710: }
711: 
712: export function cancelJob(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
713:   const resolvedDb = resolveDb(db);
714:   ensureJobTable(resolvedDb);
715:   const job = selectJob(resolvedDb, jobId);
716:   if (!job) {
717:     return null;
718:   }
719: 
720:   if (job.status === 'completed' || job.status === 'failed' || job.status === 'cancelled') {
721:     return job;
722:   }
723: 
724:   setJobStatus(resolvedDb, jobId, 'cancelled');
725:   return selectJob(resolvedDb, jobId);
726: }
727: 
728: export function resumeReindexJobs(db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob[] {
729:   const resolvedDb = resolveDb(db);
730:   requireDatabaseDir(resolvedDb, 'resume');
731:   ensureJobTable(resolvedDb);
732:   const rows = resolvedDb.prepare(`
733:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
734:     FROM embedder_jobs
735:     WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
736:     ORDER BY started_at ASC, id ASC
737:   `).all() as JobRow[];
738: 
739:   const jobs = rows.map(jobFromRow);
740:   for (const job of jobs) {
741:     enqueueJob(resolvedDb, job.id);
742:   }
743:   return jobs;
744: }
745: 
746: export function startReindexForTest(
747:   options: StartReindexOptions,
748:   runtimeOptions: ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions = {},
749: ): string {
750:   return startReindexInternal(options, runtimeOptions);
751: }
752: 
753: export function estimateEta(job: ReindexJob | null): number | null {
754:   if (!job || job.status === 'completed') {
755:     return 0;
756:   }
757:   if (job.processed <= 0 || job.total <= 0) {
758:     return null;
759:   }
760: 
761:   const elapsedMs = Date.now() - Date.parse(job.startedAt);
762:   if (!Number.isFinite(elapsedMs) || elapsedMs <= 0) {
763:     return null;
764:   }
765: 
766:   const ratePerMs = job.processed / elapsedMs;
767:   if (ratePerMs <= 0) {
768:     return null;
769:   }
770: 
771:   return Math.ceil((job.total - job.processed) / ratePerMs / 1000);
772: }

1662: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1663:    7. DATABASE UTILITIES
1664: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
1665: 
1666: /**
1667:  * Closes the shared vector-index database connection.
1668:  * @returns Nothing.
1669:  */
1670: export function close_db(): void {
1671:   clear_prepared_statements();
1672:   // C1 FIX: Close all tracked connections
1673:   for (const [, conn] of db_connections) {
1674:     try {
1675:       if (conn !== db) {
1676:         // Non-active tracked connections must also flush + TRUNCATE their
1677:         // WAL before close, exactly like the active db below — otherwise a non-active connection
1678:         // closes with only better-sqlite3's passive checkpoint and can leave un-checkpointed
1679:         // frames that an abrupt later kill could corrupt (the corruption window).
1680:         // Checkpoint BEFORE detaching the shard (the shard schema must still be attached).
1681:         try { conn.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort: shard may not be attached on this conn */ }
1682:         try { conn.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1683:         detachActiveVectorShard(conn);
1684:         conn.close();
1685:       }
1686:     } catch (_: unknown) { /* ignore close errors */ }
1687:   }
1688:   db_connections.clear();
1689:   if (db) {
1690:     const closing_db_path = db_path;
1691:     // FTS-corruption prevention: flush + TRUNCATE the
1692:     // WAL before close so context-index.sqlite is consistent at rest with an empty
1693:     // WAL. better-sqlite3 `.close()` only does a passive checkpoint and can leave
1694:     // un-checkpointed frames; an explicit TRUNCATE shrinks the window that an
1695:     // abrupt later kill (e.g. SIGKILL on MCP reconnect) could corrupt — notably
1696:     // FTS5 segment writes. Best-effort: a checkpoint failure must never block close.
1697:     try { db.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1698:     let main_checkpoint_succeeded = false;
1699:     try {
1700:       db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)');
1701:       main_checkpoint_succeeded = true;
1702:     } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1703:     // The clean-shutdown marker semantic is "present == dirty" (it is written
1704:     // on every open in initialize_db and removed only on a clean close). It must be
1705:     // deleted ONLY after a confirmed-successful close. detachActiveVectorShard() and
1706:     // db.close() can both throw; if the marker were removed before they ran (the prior
1707:     // ordering) and one of them threw, the marker would be gone while the DB did NOT
1708:     // close cleanly — so the next open would wrongly trust a dirty DB. Detach + close
1709:     // first (if either throws we never reach the removal, so the marker survives), then
1710:     // remove the marker inside the checkpoint-success guard.
1711:     detachActiveVectorShard(db);
1712:     db.close();
1713:     if (main_checkpoint_succeeded) {
1714:       remove_unclean_shutdown_marker(closing_db_path);
1715:     }
1716:     db = null;
1717:   }
1718: }
1719: 
1720: /**
1721:  * Reopens the active database around a caller-owned on-disk file swap.
1722:  *
1723:  * @param targetMainPath - Main SQLite database file to reopen after the swap.
1724:  * @param swapFn - Synchronous file swap that must run with no open connection.
1725:  * @returns The reopened active database handle.
1726:  */
1727: export function reopenActiveDatabase(targetMainPath: string, swapFn: () => void): Database.Database {
1728:   const currentDb = db ?? db_connections.get(path.resolve(targetMainPath)) ?? null;
1729: 
1730:   if (currentDb) {
1731:     try { currentDb.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1732:     try { currentDb.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1733:     detachActiveVectorShard(currentDb);
1734:   }
1735: 
1736:   close_db();
1737:   swapFn();
1738:   return initialize_db(targetMainPath, { skipRestoreRecovery: true });
1739: }
1740: 
1741: export function checkpointAllWal(): void {
1742:   if (!db) return;
1743:   try { db.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`); } catch { /* best-effort */ }
1744:   try { db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch { /* best-effort */ }
1745: }
1746:

1748:  * Gets the active vector-index database path.
1749:  * @returns The database path.
1750:  */
1751: export function get_db_path(): string {
1752:   return db_path;
1753: }

1755: /**
1756:  * Gets the shared vector-index database connection.
1757:  * @returns The database connection.
1758:  */
1759: export function get_db(): Database.Database {
1760:   return initialize_db();
1761: }

1764:  * Returns the active vector-index database connection if it has already been
1765:  * initialized. This accessor never opens SQLite or creates schema.
1766:  */

1768:   return db;
1769: }

1772: /**
1773:  * Reports whether sqlite-vec vector search is available.
1774:  * @returns True when vector search is available.
1775:  */
1776: export function is_vector_search_available(): boolean {

1780: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1781:    8. IVECTORSTORE IMPLEMENTATION
1782: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
1783: 
1784: /** Implements the vector-store interface on top of SQLite. */
1785: export class SQLiteVectorStore extends IVectorStore {
1786:   dbPath: string | null;
1787:   _initialized: boolean;
1788: 
1789:   constructor(options: { dbPath?: string } = {}) {
1790:     super();
1791:     this.dbPath = options.dbPath || null;
1792:     this._initialized = false;
1793:   }
1794: 
1795:   _ensureInitialized(): void {
1796:     if (!this._initialized) {
1797:       this._getDatabase();
1798:       this._initialized = true;
1799:     }
1800:   }
1801: 
1802:   _getDatabase(): Database.Database {
1803:     return initialize_db(this.dbPath);
1804:   }
1805: 
1806:   /**
1807:    * Searches indexed memories by embedding similarity.
1808:    * @param embedding - The query embedding to search with.
1809:    * @param topK - The maximum number of matches to return.
1810:    * @param options - Optional ranking and filtering controls.
1811:    * @returns Matching memory rows ordered by similarity.
1812:    * @throws {VectorIndexError} When the embedding dimension is invalid or the store cannot initialize.
1813:    * @example
1814:    * ```ts
1815:    * const rows = await store.search(queryEmbedding, 10, { specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
1816:    * ```
1817:    */
1818:   async search(embedding: EmbeddingInput, topK: number, options: VectorSearchOptions = {}): Promise<MemoryRow[]> {
1819:     this._ensureInitialized();
1820:     const database = this._getDatabase();
1821: 
1822:     const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
1823:     if (!embedding || embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
1824:       throw new VectorIndexError(
1825:         `Invalid embedding dimension: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${embedding?.length}`,
1826:         VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
1827:       );
1828:     }
1829: 
1830:     const search_options = {
1831:       limit: topK,
1832:       specFolder: options.specFolder,
1833:       minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity || 0,
1834:       useDecay: options.useDecay !== false,
1835:       tier: options.tier,
1836:       contextType: options.contextType,
1837:       includeConstitutional: options.includeConstitutional !== false,
1838:       includeArchived: options.includeArchived === true
1839:     };
1840: 
1841:     const { vector_search } = await getQueriesModule();
1842:     return vector_search(embedding, search_options, database);
1843:   }
1844: 
1845:   /**
1846:    * Inserts or updates a memory row and its embedding metadata.
1847:    * @param _id - Legacy identifier input retained for interface compatibility.
1848:    * @param embedding - The embedding to persist.
1849:    * @param metadata - Store metadata containing the spec folder and file path.
1850:    * @returns The stored memory identifier.
1851:    * @throws {VectorIndexError} When embedding validation fails or required metadata is missing.
1852:    * @example
1853:    * ```ts
1854:    * const id = await store.upsert('ignored', embedding, { spec_folder: 'specs/001-demo', file_path: 'spec.md' });
1855:    * ```
1856:    */
1857:   async upsert(_id: string, embedding: EmbeddingInput, metadata: JsonObject): Promise<number> {
1858:     this._ensureInitialized();
1859:     const database = this._getDatabase();
1860: 
1861:     const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
1862:     if (!embedding || embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
1863:       throw new VectorIndexError(
1864:         `Embedding dimension mismatch: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${embedding?.length}`,
1865:         VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
1866:       );
1867:     }
1868: 
1869:     const metadata_alias = metadata as JsonObject & {
1870:       spec_folder?: string;
1871:       specFolder?: string;
1872:       file_path?: string;
1873:       filePath?: string;
1874:       anchor_id?: string;
1875:       anchorId?: string;
1876:       title?: string;
1877:       trigger_phrases?: string[];
1878:       triggerPhrases?: string[];
1879:       importance_weight?: number;
1880:       importanceWeight?: number;
1881:     };
1882: 
1883:     const params: IndexMemoryParams = {
1884:       specFolder: metadata_alias.spec_folder || metadata_alias.specFolder || '',
1885:       filePath: metadata_alias.file_path || metadata_alias.filePath || '',
1886:       anchorId: metadata_alias.anchor_id || metadata_alias.anchorId || null,
1887:       title: metadata_alias.title || null,
1888:       triggerPhrases: metadata_alias.trigger_phrases || metadata_alias.triggerPhrases || [],
1889:       importanceWeight: metadata_alias.importance_weight ?? metadata_alias.importanceWeight ?? 0.5,
1890:       embedding: embedding
1891:     };
1892: 
1893:     if (!params.specFolder || !params.filePath) {
1894:       throw new VectorIndexError(
1895:         'metadata must include spec_folder and file_path',
1896:         VectorIndexErrorCode.STORE_ERROR,
1897:       );
1898:     }
1899: 
1900:     const { index_memory } = await getMutationsModule();
1901:     return index_memory(params, database);
1902:   }
1903: 
1904:   /**
1905:    * Deletes a memory by identifier.
1906:    * @param id - The memory identifier.
1907:    * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
1908:    * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates underlying delete failures from the mutation layer.
1909:    * @example
1910:    * ```ts
1911:    * const deleted = await store.delete(42);
1912:    * ```
1913:    */
1914:   async delete(id: number): Promise<boolean> {
1915:     this._ensureInitialized();
1916:     const database = this._getDatabase();
1917:     const { delete_memory } = await getMutationsModule();
1918:     return delete_memory(id, database);
1919:   }
1920: 
1921:   /**
1922:    * Retrieves a memory by identifier.
1923:    * @param id - The memory identifier.

1925:    * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates underlying store initialization failures.
1926:    * @example

1931:   async get(id: number): Promise<MemoryRow | null> {

1935:     return get_memory(id, database);

1942:     return get_stats(database);

1947:   }
1948:

1954:     if (this._initialized) {

1981: // Ensure schema version table exists and run any pending migrations
1982: /**
1983:  * Ensures the schema version table is current.
1984:  * @param database - The database connection to check.
1985:  * @returns The previous schema version.
1986:  */
1987: export function ensure_schema_version(database: Database.Database): number {
1988:   database.exec(`
1989:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
1990:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
1991:       version INTEGER NOT NULL,
1992:       updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
1993:     )
1994:   `);
1995: 
1996:   const row = database.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version WHERE id = 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
1997:   const current_version = row ? row.version : 0;
1998:   let compatibility = validate_backward_compatibility(database);
1999: 
2000:   if (current_version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
2001:     logger.info(`Migrating schema from v${current_version} to v${SCHEMA_VERSION}`);
2002:     run_migrations(database, current_version, SCHEMA_VERSION);
2003: 
2004:     compatibility = validate_backward_compatibility(database);
2005:     if (!compatibility.compatible) {
2006:       throw new Error(`Schema migration compatibility check failed: ${summarizeCompatibilityFailures(compatibility)}`);
2007:     }
2008: 
2009:     database.prepare(`
2010:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO schema_version (id, version, updated_at)
2011:       VALUES (1, ?, datetime('now'))
2012:     `).run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
2013: 
2014:     logger.info(`Schema migration complete: v${SCHEMA_VERSION}`);
2015:   }
2016: 
2017:   if (!compatibility.compatible) {
2018:     logger.warn(
2019:       'Backward-compatibility validation detected schema gaps',
2020:       compatibility as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
2021:     );
2022:   }
2023: 
2024:   initHistory(database);
2025: 
2026:   return current_version;
2027: }

2029: export function ensureLineageTables(database: Database.Database): void {
2030:   database.exec(`
2031:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_lineage (
2032:       memory_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
2033:       logical_key TEXT NOT NULL,
2034:       version_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
2035:       root_memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
2036:       predecessor_memory_id INTEGER,
2037:       superseded_by_memory_id INTEGER,
2038:       valid_from TEXT NOT NULL,
2039:       valid_to TEXT,

2144: function ensureMemoryIndexGovernanceColumns(database: Database.Database): void {
2145:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
2146:     return;
2147:   }
2148: 
2149:   const columnNames = getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index');
2150: 
2151:   const governanceColumns: Array<{ name: string; sql: string }> = [
2152:     { name: 'tenant_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT' },
2153:     { name: 'user_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN user_id TEXT' },
2154:     { name: 'agent_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN agent_id TEXT' },
2155:     { name: 'provenance_source', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN provenance_source TEXT' },
2156:     { name: 'provenance_actor', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN provenance_actor TEXT' },
2157:     { name: 'source_kind', sql: SOURCE_KIND_COLUMN_SQL },
2158:     { name: 'governed_at', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN governed_at TEXT' },
2159:     { name: 'retention_policy', sql: "ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN retention_policy TEXT DEFAULT 'keep'" },
2160:     { name: 'delete_after', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN delete_after TEXT' },
2161:     { name: 'governance_metadata', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN governance_metadata TEXT' },
2162:   ];
2163: 
2164:   for (const column of governanceColumns) {
2165:     if (columnNames.includes(column.name)) continue;
2166:     try {
2167:       database.exec(column.sql);
2168:     } catch (error: unknown) {
2169:       logDuplicateColumnMigrationSkip(column.name, error);
2170:     }
2171:   }
2172: 
2173:   ensureMemoryIndexSourceKindColumn(database, 'governance bootstrap');
2174: }

2176: // Idempotent: runs on every startup. If the column is gone we return without
2177: // touching the database. If DROP COLUMN throws (older SQLite), we swallow the
2178: // error and leave the orphan column in place; the runtime never reads or
2179: // writes it either way.
2180: function dropDeprecatedSharedSpaceColumn(database: Database.Database): void {
2181:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) return;
2182:   const columnNames = getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index');
2183:   if (!columnNames.includes('shared_space_id')) return;
2184:   try {
2185:     database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN shared_space_id');
2186:   } catch {
2187:     // Older SQLite without DROP COLUMN support: leave the column in place.
2188:   }
2189: }

2229: /**
2230:  * Validates backward compatibility expectations for the current schema.
2231:  * Never throws; returns compatibility details for logging and rollout gates.
2232:  */
2233: export function validate_backward_compatibility(database: Database.Database): SchemaCompatibilityReport {
2234:   try {
2235:     const missingTables = REQUIRED_TABLES.filter((tableName) => !hasTable(database, tableName));
2236:     const missingColumns: Record<string, string[]> = {};
2237:     const missingIndexes: string[] = [];
2238:     const constraintMismatches: string[] = [];
2239:     const warnings: string[] = [];
2240: 
2241:     if (hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
2242:       const existingColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index'));
2243:       const absentColumns = REQUIRED_MEMORY_INDEX_COLUMNS.filter((columnName) => !existingColumns.has(columnName));
2244:       if (absentColumns.length > 0) {
2245:         missingColumns.memory_index = absentColumns;
2246:       }
2247:       if (!hasConstitutionalTierConstraint(database)) {
2248:         constraintMismatches.push('memory_index.importance_tier CHECK constraint is missing constitutional support');
2249:       }
2250:     } else {
2251:       missingColumns.memory_index = [...REQUIRED_MEMORY_INDEX_COLUMNS];
2252:     }
2253: 
2254:     if (hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
2255:       const existingColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_conflicts'));
2256:       const absentColumns = REQUIRED_MEMORY_CONFLICT_COLUMNS.filter((columnName) => !existingColumns.has(columnName));
2257:       if (absentColumns.length > 0) {
2258:         missingColumns.memory_conflicts = absentColumns;
2259:       }
2260:     }
2261: 
2262:     if (hasTable(database, 'causal_edge_tombstones')) {
2263:       const existingColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'causal_edge_tombstones'));
2264:       const absentColumns = REQUIRED_CAUSAL_EDGE_TOMBSTONE_COLUMNS.filter((columnName) => !existingColumns.has(columnName));
2265:       if (absentColumns.length > 0) {
2266:         missingColumns.causal_edge_tombstones = absentColumns;
2267:       }
2268:     } else {
2269:       missingColumns.causal_edge_tombstones = [...REQUIRED_CAUSAL_EDGE_TOMBSTONE_COLUMNS];
2270:     }
2271: 
2272:     if (hasTable(database, 'memory_trigger_embeddings')) {
2273:       const existingColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_trigger_embeddings'));
2274:       const absentColumns = REQUIRED_TRIGGER_EMBEDDING_COLUMNS.filter((columnName) => !existingColumns.has(columnName));
2275:       if (absentColumns.length > 0) {
2276:         missingColumns.memory_trigger_embeddings = absentColumns;
2277:       }
2278:     } else {
2279:       missingColumns.memory_trigger_embeddings = [...REQUIRED_TRIGGER_EMBEDDING_COLUMNS];
2280:     }
2281: 
2282:     if (hasTable(database, 'memory_idempotency_receipts')) {
2283:       const existingColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_idempotency_receipts'));
2284:       const absentColumns = REQUIRED_IDEMPOTENCY_RECEIPT_COLUMNS.filter((columnName) => !existingColumns.has(columnName));
2285:       if (absentColumns.length > 0) {
2286:         missingColumns.memory_idempotency_receipts = absentColumns;
2287:       }
2288:     } else {
2289:       missingColumns.memory_idempotency_receipts = [...REQUIRED_IDEMPOTENCY_RECEIPT_COLUMNS];
2290:     }
2291: 
2292:     for (const [tableName, requiredIndexes] of Object.entries(REQUIRED_INDEXES_BY_TABLE)) {
2293:       if (!hasTable(database, tableName)) {
2294:         continue;
2295:       }
2296:       for (const indexName of requiredIndexes) {
2297:         if (!hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
2298:           missingIndexes.push(indexName);
2299:         }
2300:       }
2301:     }
2302: 
2303:     if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_history')) {
2304:       warnings.push('memory_history table missing; historical replay functionality may be degraded.');
2305:     }
2306:     if (!hasTable(database, 'checkpoints')) {
2307:       warnings.push('checkpoints table missing; migration checkpoint tooling may be unavailable.');
2308:     }
2309:     if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
2310:       warnings.push('memory_conflicts table missing; conflict audit trail may be incomplete.');
2311:     }
2312: 
2313:     const schemaVersion = safe_get_schema_version(database);
2314:     return {
2315:       compatible: (
2316:         missingTables.length === 0
2317:         && Object.keys(missingColumns).length === 0
2318:         && missingIndexes.length === 0
2319:         && constraintMismatches.length === 0
2320:       ),
2321:       schemaVersion,
2322:       missingTables,
2323:       missingColumns,
2324:       missingIndexes,
2325:       constraintMismatches,
2326:       warnings,
2327:     };
2328:   } catch (error: unknown) {
2329:     return {
2330:       compatible: false,
2331:       schemaVersion: null,
2332:       missingTables: [...REQUIRED_TABLES],
2333:       missingColumns: {
2334:         memory_index: [...REQUIRED_MEMORY_INDEX_COLUMNS],
2335:         memory_trigger_embeddings: [...REQUIRED_TRIGGER_EMBEDDING_COLUMNS],
2336:       },
2337:       missingIndexes: [],
2338:       constraintMismatches: ['compatibility inspection failed before constraint verification'],
2339:       warnings: [
2340:         `Compatibility check failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
2341:       ],
2342:     };
2343:   }
2344: }

2346: export function validateLineageSchemaSupport(database: Database.Database): LineageSchemaReport {
2347:   try {
2348:     const missingTables = REQUIRED_LINEAGE_TABLES.filter((tableName) => !hasTable(database, tableName));
2349:     const missingColumns: Record<string, string[]> = {};
2350:     const warnings: string[] = [];
2351: 
2352:     for (const [tableName, requiredColumns] of Object.entries(REQUIRED_LINEAGE_COLUMNS)) {
2353:       if (!hasTable(database, tableName)) {
2354:         missingColumns[tableName] = [...requiredColumns];
2355:         continue;
2356:       }
2357: 
2358:       const existingColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, tableName));
2359:       const absentColumns = requiredColumns.filter((columnName) => !existingColumns.has(columnName));
2360:       if (absentColumns.length > 0) {
2361:         missingColumns[tableName] = [...absentColumns];
2362:       }
2363:     }
2364: 
2365:     if (!hasTable(database, 'checkpoints')) {
2366:       warnings.push('checkpoints table missing; rollback drills cannot be validated safely.');
2367:     }
2368:     if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_history')) {
2369:       warnings.push('memory_history table missing; lineage bridge metadata will be incomplete.');
2370:     }
2371: 
2372:     return {
2373:       compatible: missingTables.length === 0 && Object.keys(missingColumns).length === 0,
2374:       schemaVersion: safe_get_schema_version(database),
2375:       missingTables,
2376:       missingColumns,
2377:       warnings,
2378:     };
2379:   } catch (error: unknown) {
2380:     const missingColumns: Record<string, string[]> = {};
2381:     for (const [tableName, requiredColumns] of Object.entries(REQUIRED_LINEAGE_COLUMNS)) {
2382:       missingColumns[tableName] = [...requiredColumns];
2383:     }
2384:     return {
2385:       compatible: false,
2386:       schemaVersion: null,
2387:       missingTables: [...REQUIRED_LINEAGE_TABLES],
2388:       missingColumns,
2389:       warnings: [
2390:         `Lineage compatibility check failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
2391:       ],
2392:     };
2393:   }
2394: }

2396: function safe_get_schema_version(database: Database.Database): number | null {
2397:   try {
2398:     if (!hasTable(database, 'schema_version')) {
2399:       return null;
2400:     }
2401:     const row = database.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version WHERE id = 1').get() as { version?: number } | undefined;
2402:     if (typeof row?.version === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.version)) {
2403:       return row.version;
2404:     }
2405:     return null;
2406:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
2407:     return null;
2408:   }
2409: }

1533:   }
